Centre for Yoga and Holistic wellness
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Kasthurba A. K.
CONTACT
Email id:
chair.cyhw@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2286901, 9946006309
Mailing Address
Dr. Kasthurba A. K.
Centre for Yoga and Holistic Wellness
National Institute of Technology Calicut, NIT Campus P O, Kozhikode 673601
In line with the National Education Policy 2020, NITC has established the Centre for Yoga and Holistic Wellness (CYHW) to promote Yoga and Wellness training and research
Mission
To enable and empower students, faculties and staff tto enhance their capability in academics, research and profession through yoga and practice leading to a health conscious community, contributing to the society and nation building.
Vision
To empower academic community through Yoga and Mindfulness Practice for holistic wellness to address challenges in profession and life.
Collaborations
Indian Yoga Association (An industry cum - self regulatory body of leading yoga institute and organisation in India)
Patanjali Yoga Research Centre, Kozhikode

PROFILE
Chemistry
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2009
Head of the Department:
Dr. Parameswaran Pattiyil
CONTACT
Email id:
chemistry@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 495 2285301
Mailing Address
Department of Chemistry
National Institute of Technology Calicut
Kerala
Pin - 673601
India
In education and research, the department is emerging as a major centre of excellence, not only in the national level but also in the international level. The academic activities of the department are supported by highly qualified and experienced faculties who pursue research in the frontier areas of science. The major research areas covered by the department include theoretical and computational chemistry, polymer science and technology, physical chemistry, coordination chemistry, organic chemistry, bio-organic and bio-inorganic chemistry. The department has the state of the art instrumentation facilities, for thermal, mechanical and spectroscopic characterization of materials, and other laboratory facilities. The research activities in the department are generously supported by the external national and international funding agencies. The department also has collaborative research programs with national and international universities/institutes.
Vision
High quality performance in chemical education and training.
Mission
Create an ambience of academic excellence in chemical science and technology in compliance with the vision and mission of NIT Calicut.

PROFILE
Architecture and Planning
OVERVIEW
Established in:
1999
Head of the Department:
Dr. Mohammed Firoz C.
CONTACT
Email id:
hodarc@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2286901
Mailing Address
Department of Architecture and Planning,
National Institute of Technology, Calicut NIT Campus
Post, Kozhikode, Kerala - 673 601.
The Department of Architecture and Planning is committed to providing an academically strong platform for those aspirants who would have a promising career and leave an enduring impression in the field of Architecture and Urban Planning.
The educational program in Architecture throughout the world shares a common view of the future of mankind and recognises its unpredictability. This calls for an attitude of research, exploration and evaluation to authenticate the work of design. Great are the thoughts that moved the world... greater are the initiators of these thoughts. The Department of Architecture and Planning plans and strives to reach these goals by offering the Undergraduate (5 Year B.Arch.) Degree Program in Architecture.
The Post Graduate Degree (2 Year M. Plan) Program in Urban Planning aims to produce generalist planning professionals of international quality who can adapt to any challenging planning situation with superior capability to use geo-informatics which includes GIS, remote sensing, related models and quantitative methods in urban, regional and environmental planning. The program envisages inculcating scientific diagnostic and urban management abilities in professional planners to understand planning issues holistically and equip them with predictive ability to analyse the outcome of economic, social, environmental and energy impacts using simulation of future scenarios. Additionally, the department boasts of a PhD program with over 100 broad research areas.
The Department has achieved a rare feat of achieving the SECOND position In the NIRF rankings for Architecture and Planning consecutively for three years - 2023, 2022 & 2021. It secured the THIRD position in the rankings in the years 2020 and 2019, respectively.
Previously,
the Department of Architecture and Planning has been ranked FIFTH in India among the Best Schools of Architecture by the Outlook Survey 2017. This was FOURTH time in a row (from 2014 onwards) the Department is maintaining its position in the TOP TEN Schools in India. The Department was ranked SIXTH in the year 2016 and 2015 in the Outlook Survey.
Vision
To develop professional expertise and proficient scholarship in architecture, planning and allied disciplines, ingrained with social and environmental responsibility through interdisciplinary knowledge integration and cutting-edge research.
Mission
To advance and disseminate approaches to address complex challenges faced by the society at large, by equipping students to actively participate, anticipate and respond to the contemporary built environment milieu.
Please visit the following link for details:
Centre of Excellence for Urban Planning and Design by Government of India
NIRF 2023 RANKINGS:
MoE, National Institute Ranking Framework (NIRF) (nirfindia.org)
NIRF 2022 RANKINGS:
MoE, National Institute Ranking Framework (NIRF) (nirfindia.org)
NIRF 2021 RANKINGS:
MoE, National Institute Ranking Framework (NIRF) (nirfindia.org)
OUTLOOK:
http://www.outlookindia.com/magazine/story/top-architecture-colleges-in-2017/298921
Linked In : www.linkedin.com/in/dapnitc
Twitter/ X : @dapsocial_nitc

PROFILE
Physics
OVERVIEW
Head of the Department:
Dr. Vari Sivaji Reddy
CONTACT
Website:
https://depcent.nitc.ac.in/physics/
Email id:
hodphysics@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 495 228 5100
Mailing Address
Department of Physics,
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
Kozhikode, Kerala, INDIA 673 601
Fax : +91 495 228 7201
The Department of Physics in NITC is emerging as a major centre for teaching and research in applied physics and technology, with a dynamic team of faculty members, technical staff and research scholars. In addition to the Bachelors and Masters programmes (B.Tech. Engineering Physics and M.Sc. Physics), the department has an active and vibrant research programmes in experimental, theoretical and computational physics, supported by several externally funded projects.
Vision
To be a centre of international repute for higher learning and research in fundamental and applied physics.
Mission
Develop personnel with sound knowledge in all aspects of fundamental physics, applied physics and technology with a strong potential for research, innovation, leadership and entrepreneurship for the betterment of humankind.
Announcements:
A five day GIAN course on
SOFT MATTER IN PHYSICS, BIOLOGY AND MATERIALS SCIENCES: PROGRAMMABILITY, ACTUATION AND RECONFIGURABILITY
to be held at Department of Physics, NIT Calicut from
24th to 28th November 2025:
Brochure
Applications are invited for admissions to the Ph.D. programme (Monsoon Semester 2025-26). Deadline: 17th April 2025, 5.00 PM.
Deadline extended to 24 April 2025, 5.00PM

Apr 16, 2025
Indian Army Unveils India’s First Geothermal-Based Net-Zero Energy Building: A Landmark Step Towards Sustainable Defense Infrastructure
The Indian Army has demonstrated its commitment to environmental stewardship and sustainable innovation with the construction of India’s first geothermal-based net-zero energy and off-grid building by MES Jhansi under the aegis of Headquarters Chief Engineer, Bhopal Zone. This landmark project was officially validated as a Net-Zero building by the team of
Centre for Clean Energy & Circular Economy, National Institute of Technology Calicut.
The team of Prof.A.Shaija, Dr.Vinod Kumar Sharma and Dr.S.N.Deepa visited CWE, Jhansi during 26
th
- 28
th
March 2025 and 16
th
– 18
th
April 2025 and performed the training and validation of the net zero building.
This building taps into Earth's stable underground temperatures through a closed-loop system comprising ten boreholes, each 120 meters deep. High-density polyethylene piping circulates a natural refrigerant, allowing efficient heat exchange with the ground without emissions, contamination, or contributing to urban heat island effects. The building’s heating and cooling systems revolve around a high-efficiency ground-source heat pump. This system uses radiant floor heating during winters and ceiling-mounted fan coil units for summer cooling, providing optimal comfort throughout the year.
During colder months, the building’s floor slab acts as a thermal mass, storing and radiating heat evenly, minimizing convective losses. Additionally, the Mechanical Ventilation with Heat Recovery (MVHR) system ensures continuous fresh air circulation while reclaiming sensible heat from outgoing air supporting superior indoor air quality with minimal thermal loss. The system’s intelligence lies in its collector manifold, which balances hydraulic loads and modulates temperature gradients across the geothermal loops using integrated sensors.
Complementing its geothermal backbone, the building generates more renewable energy via rooftop solar panels than it consumes annually registering an impressive EPI below 35. The project offsets a substantial volume of CO₂ emissions compared to conventional buildings, marking another stride by the Indian Army toward self-sufficiency, energy independence, and carbon-neutral infrastructure.

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
Aug 17, 2024
NIT Calicut Welcomes Over 2300 New Students in Grand Orientation Program
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NIT Calicut) successfully conducted an expansive orientation program to welcome its new undergraduate (UG), postgraduate (PG), and Ph.D. scholars for the academic year 2024-2025. The event witnessed the participation of more than 2300 new students, including over 1300 UG students, over 800 PG students, and over 200 Ph.D. scholars, along with their parents.
The UG orientation program was inaugurated by Shri Snehil Kumar Singh, District Collector of Kozhikode and a distinguished alumnus of IIT Roorkee’s 2014 batch. In his inspiring address, the District Collector emphasized the importance of holistic development, urging students to balance academic achievements with extracurricular pursuits.
He remarked, “Scoring grades is not the only measure of success. Exams do not define your worth. What matters is how you utilize your knowledge and skills.” Shri. Snehil Kumar Singh also called on students to be "carbon conscious" and to channel their expertise toward addressing global challenges, especially considering the limited resources available. He stressed the need for innovation across all sectors, stating, "India is looking to you for solutions."
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut, echoed the importance of personal growth, advising new UG students to focus on self-improvement rather than competing with others. "The competition should be with oneself to strive for continuous betterment," he added.
The PG orientation program was inaugurated by Prof. J. N. Moorthy, Director of IISER, who highlighted the critical role of the intersection between science, technology, and society. In his keynote speech, Prof. Moorthy underscored how advancements in these fields contribute significantly to national security, social welfare, and overall development. He lauded the growth of India’s higher education sector, noting the expansion from 21 universities in 1947 to 1,265 today, alongside over 50,000 colleges nationwide.
Addressing the PG students, Prof. Prasad Krishna urged them to transcend the boundaries of their specific programs and rise to the challenges posed by contemporary global issues. He emphasized the pivotal role of the youth in achieving the vision of a developed India by 2047.
The orientation program also featured addresses by Dr. A.V. Babu, Dean (Academic); Cdr. Dr. M.S. Shamasundara, Registrar; Dr. Raju Dey and Dr. Preeti Navaneet, General Convenors of the UG/PG Orientation.
Subsequent sessions included insights from Dr. Sathyananda Panda, Dean (Students’ Welfare); Dr. K. Haribabu, Chairperson, Council of Wardens; Dr. N. Sandhyarani, Dean (Research & Consultancy); Dr. Sudheer A.P., Chairperson, CIEI; Dr. Arun F. Adrakatti, Deputy Librarian; Dr. Praveen Sankaran, Chairperson, Centre for Career Development; Dr. M. Yogesh Kumar and Dr. Vrinda Varma, Faculty Coordinators of the Student Guidance Cell; Dr. Ravi Varma, Dean (IACR); and Dr. Arun P., Convenor of the Healthcare and Wellness Committee.
This comprehensive orientation marked the beginning of an exciting academic journey for the new students, equipping them with the necessary insights and inspiration to navigate their time at NIT Calicut effectively.
Shri Snehil Kumar Singh, District Collector, Kozhikode, arriving for the inauguration of the UG Orientation programme with Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut and Cdr. Dr. M S Shamasundara, Registrar of NITC.
District Collector Shri Snehil Kumar Singh inaugurated the UG Orientation Programme at NIT Calicut.
District Collector Shri Snehil Kumar Singh released the Swagatham UG and PG Handbook.

PROFILE
Civil Engineering
OVERVIEW
Established in:
1961
Head of the Department:
Dr. T. M. Madhavan Pillai
CONTACT
Email id:
civilhod@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 495 2286200
Mailing Address
Department of Civil Engineering,
NIT Calicut, NIT Campus P. O.,
Calicut - 673601.
Welcome to the website of Department of Civil Engineering.
The Department of Civil Engineering is one of the oldest Departments in this Institute. It was established at the inception stage of the Calicut Regional Engineering College (CREC), which was the forerunner to the present National Institute of Technology, Calicut (NITC) in 1961. Over the years, the Department has grown many times in different spheres of activities. At present, the Department offers an undergraduate programme in Civil Engineering leading to the BTech degree and six graduate programmes - one each in Structural Engineering, Traffic and Transportation Planning, Offshore Structures, Geotechnical Engineering, Water Resources Engineering and Environmental Engineering leading to the MTech degree of the Institute. In addition to the above, there are a number of students pursuing research at the Department in various fields of Civil Engineering leading to PhD. The Department is a recognized QIP centre of the AICTE for both MTech and PhD programmes. In addition to the above courses, the Department has been regularly conducting a number of short-term training programmes for the benefit of faculty of technical institutions and working professionals.
The Department is also actively engaged in R&D, testing and consultancy activities. A number of R&D projects sponsored by various departments and organisations including the Ministry of Human Resources Development (MHRD) and the Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, AICTE, ADRB, NPOL, Coir Board, and the Kerala State Council for Science Technology and Environment (KSCSTE) have been completed and many are presently in progress. The Department has offered and continues to offer testing and consultancy services to various government departments and organizations in both the public and private sectors. The Government of Kerala has approved the Department as an authority for checking and scrutinising designs of public works. The Department is the State Technical Agency of National Rural Roads Development Agency, Government of India. Consultancy services are being offered in all fields of Civil Engineering interest.
The laboratories of the Department are well equipped. A major modernisation of various laboratories in the Department was taken up under the Technical Education Quality Improvement Program (TEQIP) of the Ministry of Human Resources Development (MHRD), Government of India, during 2002-2006. The next phase of this programme is presently being taken up.
The Department also has to its credit the Centre for Transportation Research (CTR), a Centre of Excellence funded by the Ministry of Human Resources Development, under the scheme ‘Establishment of 50 Centres of Excellence in frontier areas of Science and Technology’. The Centre takes up research, education, training and outreach activities in the areas of Transportation Planning, Traffic Engineering and Pavement Technology.
Detailed information on faculty and their expertise, laboratories, activities of the Department etc. are available in the web pages. In case you are unable to find any information pertaining to the Department that is of interest to you, please feel free to contact us.
Vision
The Vision of the Department of Civil Engineering is to be a world class academic centre for quality education and research in diverse areas of Civil Engineering, with a strong social commitment.
Mission
The mission of the department is to
Impart quality education in undergraduate and post graduate levels, with strong emphasis on professional ethics and social commitment
Provide a scholastic environment for state – of –art research, resulting in practical applications
Produce highly competent and technologically capable professionals and motivated young academicians
Undertake professional consultancy services in diverse areas of Civil Engineering
Conduct knowledge exchange programmes with various stakeholders

PROFILE
Equal Opportunity Cell
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. M. S. Sunitha
CONTACT
Email id:
eoc@nitc.ac.in
India is potentially rich in human resources. To harvest the same and make the present education system inclusive, the degree of democratization of higher education has to be increased to a large extent. Further, institutes should become more responsive to the needs and constraints of the disadvantaged social groups such as SCs, STs, women, OBC (non-creamy layer), minorities and physically challenged persons. Therefore, Equal Opportunity Cell (EOC) has been established in institutes.
Aims and Objectives
To oversee the effective implementation of policies and programmes for disadvantaged groups, to provide guidance and counselling with respect to academic, financial, social and other matters and to enhance the diversity within the campus.

RESEARCH & CONSULTANCY
OUTREACH ACTIVITIES
NITC Director Prof. Prasad Krishna inaugurated the 'Science and Technology Week' programme at CWRDM, Kozhikode
NITC Director Prof. Prasad Krishna participated as Chief  Guest in a programme conducted at A.U.P School, Malayamma to honour the teachers in Kozhikode District who developed online tools for  teaching and learning.
Dr. Rajanikant G K, Professor of  Biotechnology & Dean (SW), has been appointed as the Chairperson of the Ethics Committee, Department of Homeopathy, Govt. of Kerala
Civil Engineering Department of NITC supported the "Sky Walk" project of Calicut Medical College as a Social Outreach activity.
Dr. C B Sobhan, Professor, SMSE, has been nominated as the Joint Secretary of the prestigious Indian Society of Heat and Mass and Transfer (ISHMT). Earlier he was member of the Executive Committee.
Dr. A Shaija, Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering has been nominated as a member of the Senate in Indian Institute of Information Technology Design and Manufacturing (lllTDM), Kurnool, an Institute of National importance under Government of India, on August 2021 for a period of two years.
Deputy Director Dr. Sathidevi P S participated as the Guest of Honour at the Convocation of MDIT, Koyilandi

Right to Information
Home
About RTI
Disclosures
Reports
Links
Guidelines
RTI
How to file RTI application ?
Online Mode
Visit
https://rtionline.gov.in
and follow the instructions provided therein. User manual and online payment facility are available in this portal.
Physical Mode
Download the Application (
doc
,
pdf
) and send the duly-filled application along with the application fee of Rs.10/-.
The application has to be sent to
The Public Information Officer, National Institute of Technology Calicut, NIT Campus P.O., Kozhikode, Kerala-673601.
RTI-Application Fee
Payment of Application Fee (for physical mode)
The application fee of Rs.10/- in the form of
Demand Draft
or
Bankers Cheque
or
Indian Postal Order
drawn in favour of The Director, NIT Calicut.
Application fee can be remitted directly at the Counter available at Accounts Section under the Head
RTI-Application Fee
and the receipt has to be attached together with the RTI Application.
If the applicant belongs to below poverty line (BPL) category, he is not required to pay any fee. However, he should submit a proof in support of his claim for belonging to the below poverty line.
Court Stamps and any other mode of payments are not acceptable.
Officers designated under RTI Act 2005
Public Information Officer
Ms. Nilina Elais.
Assistant Registrar (Establishment)
National Institute of Technology Calicut
NIT Campus P.O., Kozhikode
Kerala Pincode: 673601
Appellate Authority
Dr. Babu A. V.
Dean (Academic)
National Institute of Technology Calicut
NIT Campus P.O., Kozhikode
Kerala Pincode: 673601

VISITOR
Her Excellency Smt. Droupadi Murmu
Honourable President of India
Smt. Droupadi Murmu was sworn in as the 15th President of India on 25 July, 2022. Previously, she was the Governor of Jharkhand from 2015 to 2021. She has devoted her life to empowering the downtrodden as well as the marginalised sections of society and deepening the democratic values.
Born in a Santhali tribal family on 20 June, 1958 at Uparbeda village, Mayurbhanj, Odisha, Smt. Murmu’s early life was marked by hardships and struggle. On completion of primary education from the village school, she went to Bhubaneswar on her own initiative to continue her studies. She earned the degree of Bachelor of Arts from Ramadevi Women’s College, Bhubaneswar and became the first woman from her village to receive college education.
Read more
Smt. Droupadi Murmu
15th President of India.

Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Women Welfare and Social Empowerment (CWSE)
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. S. Bhuvaneshwari
CONTACT
Website:
https://cwse.nitc.ac.in
Mailing Address
Chairperson
Centre for Women Welfare and Social Empowerment (CWSE)
NIT Calicut
673601
Women empowerment has been identified by the Government of India as a prerequisite not only for family and societal development, but also for the entire nation building and sustainable world. Affirmative legislations, plans and systematic programs are introduced in recent times by the Government with support from NGOs and Corporate Social Responsibility initiatives to cater the diverse domains constituting the United Nations identified Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
India has now endorsed the global effort of women welfare along with its preparations to achieve the UN sustainable development goal SDG-5 in a fast- track progression by 2030 itself. Hence there is an urgent need of assessing the above-mentioned affirmative actions, giving fresh impetus on empowering girls and women not only through conventional education but also hand holding for skill acquisition and skill development and providing them with equitable economic and social opportunities.
As a premier higher education institute in engineering, technology and scientific domain, National Institute of Technology Calicut is rightly positioned to support the women welfare and empowerment drive in the multiple segments the government have identified. The present proposal clearly identifies the various segments which directly constitutes with the women empowerment drive and proposes new programs, measures and action steps to implement the fast-track action plan. For the systematic implementation, continuous operation and regular monitoring and assessment and feedback measures aimed at societal development, a new dedicated Centre for Women Welfare and Social Empowerment is established at the institute.
Vision
An inclusive space for women of all backgrounds, ages, and experiences, providing workshops, training programs, and mentorship opportunities to help women develop leadership, communication, and entrepreneurial skills.
Mission
Provide a variety of programs and services to help women develop their skills, knowledge, and confidence through public speaking, financial literacy, networking, and digital skills. Deliver resources and services to support women's physical, emotional, and mental well-being. Establish a space that empowers and uplifts women, equipping them with the expertise and knowledge they need to succeed in all areas of life.
Objectives
As an institute of national importance, NIT Calicut can help to assure women's empowerment in multiple ways. Encourage diversity and inclusion in the NITC community is a major part of this initiative. By promoting a diverse and
inclusive culture at NIT Calicut, the institute can help to create a welcoming and supportive environment for women and other underrepresented groups. Initiatives such as providing scholarships, mentorship programs, and support
services can help women succeed academically and professionally. The major objectives of the Centre for Women Welfare and Social Empowerment, CWSE include, but not limited to the following :
Encourage women's leadership and participation
Advocate for gender equality
Promote a culture of lifelong learning
Offer technical training and professional development programmes
Offer health, wellness and self-defence training sessions
Partner with industry and organisations
Provide mentorship, coaching and engage women in entrepreneurship.
Visit Website

Multidisciplinary Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Materials Characterization (CMC)
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. Maneesh Chandran
CONTACT
Email id:
maneesh@nitc.ac.in / cmcadmin@nitc.ac.in
Mailing Address
The Centre for Materials Characterization (CMC) at NIT Calicut is a premier research facility equipped with advanced instruments including X-ray Diffraction (XRD), Raman Spectroscopy, Vector Network Analyzer (VNA), Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR), and a Liquid Nitrogen Plant. Serving as a multidisciplinary hub, the CMC supports cutting-edge research and academic activities across various domains of science and engineering. It provides essential infrastructure for precise material analysis, enabling both in-house and external researchers to conduct high-quality investigations. The Centre is committed to fostering innovation, collaboration, and technical excellence within the institute and beyond, and its facilities are open to the NIT community as well as other academic institutions, industries, and R&D laboratories.
CMC - Facilities
•
Powder X-ray diffractometer
•
Confocal Raman Spectrometer
•
500 MHz Nuclear Magnetic Resonance Spectrometer
• Liquid nitrogen generator

Multidisciplinary Centres
Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Electronics & Communication Engineering
OVERVIEW
Established in:
1980
Head of the Department:
Dr. G. Sreelekha
CONTACT
Email id:
hodeced@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2286701
Mailing Address
Department of Electronics & Communication Engineering,

National Institute of Technology Calicut
NIT Campus P.O. CALICUT-673601
The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, National Institute of Technology, Calicut, is involved in providing quality education at both Undergraduate (UG) and Postgraduate (PG) levels. UG programme in Electronics & Communication Engineering started in 1980 in the Department of Electrical Engineering. In 1994 a PG programme in Digital Systems and Communication started. The rapid development in Electronics and Communications initiated the inception of a separate Department of Electronics Engineering in 1997, after the trifurcation of the Department of Electrical Engineering. In addition to the above programmes the Department is offering PG programmes in Electronic Design & Technology, Microelectronics & VLSI Design, Telecommunication and Signal Processing. The syllabi of the courses are continuously updated and the laboratories modernized to reflect the rapid changes in technology. It also offers high quality research programmes at Ph.D. level in a variety of areas.
Vision
The Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering is envisioned to be a leading centre of higher learning with academic excellence in the field of electronics and communication engineering.
Mission
The mission of the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering is to impart high quality technical education by offering undergraduate, graduate and research programs in the domain of electronics and communication engineering with thorough foundation in theory, along with strong hands-on design and laboratory components, tools and skills necessary for the students to become successful major contributors to society and profession.
Department faculty

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
Aug 12, 2024
NIT Calicut Shines in National Rankings, Continues Legacy of Excellence
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NIT Calicut) has once again solidified its position as a leader in higher education in India, demonstrating exceptional performance in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF) 2024 rankings. The institute’s consistent excellence across various disciplines underscores its unwavering commitment to quality education, innovative research, and the holistic development of students.
In the latest rankings, NIT Calicut's engineering programs have been ranked 25th nationally, reaffirming the institute's status as one of the top five National Institutes of Technology (NITs) in the country. This achievement is a testament to institute's dedication to maintaining high academic standards and fostering an environment conducive to learning and innovation.
A standout achievement for NIT Calicut is the stellar performance of its Department of Architecture, which has secured the 3rd position nationwide. This places NIT Calicut as the top performer among the 31 NITs in the field of Architecture, surpassing even several older generation Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs). Remarkably, NIT Calicut is the only NIT to rank within the top three in this category, highlighting its leadership in architectural education and planning.
The NIRF rankings serve as a reflection of the highly competitive landscape of higher education in India, and NIT Calicut's sustained efforts to elevate its academic standards are evident. The institute continues to hold the distinction of being the top engineering institution in Kerala, excelling in parameters such as teaching, learning, and graduate outcomes, among others. These achievements underscore NIT Calicut’s ability to deliver exceptional results year after year.
“NIT Calicut’s strong academic foundation is built on a rich history of excellence, a highly qualified faculty, and a vibrant student community,” said Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut. “As we move forward, the institute will continue to focus on cutting-edge research, industry collaboration, and the overall development of our students, ensuring that we remain at the forefront of higher education in India.”
Key factors contributing to NIT Calicut’s success include significant placement rates, competitive pay packages for graduates, opportunities for higher studies, and an increase in the number of qualified permanent faculty members and their research outputs. The institute’s ongoing efforts in these areas reflect its commitment to nurturing future leaders and innovators who will contribute to the nation’s growth and development.

COLLABORATING AND INNOVATING
A big family — students, faculty, staff and alumni — representing the institution with pride. NITC students, faculty and staff live active, engaged lives of teaching, learning and making an impact within a diverse and supportive community.
NITC's mission to advance knowledge and discovery benefits from the presence of an intellectually stimulating mix of voices and ideas. We are a teaching and learning community grounded in a respect for the views of others. That mix of perspectives and commitment to respect — coupled with the innovative, dynamic spirit of our community — infuse life at NITC in settings that range from academic to residential to social.
Members of the NITC community collaborate on pioneering research, innovative startups, artistic performance and social actions. We are a community that celebrates deep intellectual curiosity, creativity and individuality.
STUDENTS AFFAIRS COUNCIL
Students are the life and blood of any educational institution. They form the vast majority of the campus inmates and the success of any institution depends, to a large extent, on the proper administration of the requirement of the student community. The students in turn have the boundan duty to acquire the knowledge and skills as laid out in the curriculum to become socially useful and productive citizens. The students’ council, popularly called ‘Student’s Affairs Council’ is the voice of the student community. It acts as the bridge between the students and the administrations of the institution. It also provides a platform for the students to tone up their leadership and communication skills. It shall conduct in a democratic manner honouring dignity and freedom of all who would associate with it
The most prominent role of the Student’s Council, however is its endeavor to ensure student satisfaction and academic progress in the institution, without which no educational institution can progress. It acts as the representative body of the student community to bring forth duly the requirements of the student to the authorities. A Students’ Affairs Council, which has powers delegated on it, can go a long way if the general welfare of the student community is properly taken care of. It would in turn organize activities for the overall development and academic excellence of students.

PROFILE
Centre for Quality Assurance and Enhancement
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Lity Alen Varghese
CONTACT
Website:
https://depcent.nitc.ac.in/cqae/
Email id:
chair.cqae@nitc.ac.in
Mailing Address
Chairperson, Centre for Q.A and Enhancement, NIT Calicut - 673601
Vision
Create an ethos towards holistic quality enhancement in academic, research and administrative practices of the Institute.
Mission
Identify and apply benchmarks for assessment of quality in academic, research and administrative spheres in the Institute.
Develop a system for conscious, consistent and catalytic action to improve the performance by the conduct of audits in Academics, Research, Administration, Equipment utilisation, Energy consumption and Environmental sustainability.
Promote measures for institutional functioning towards quality enhancement through internalisation of quality culture and institutionalisation of best practices.
Function as a nodal agency to ensure continuous improvement of quality culture in the Institute.
Visit Website

Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Holistic Teaching and Learning
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. A. Sujith
CONTACT
Mailing Address
Introduction
A Center for Holistic Teaching-Learning (CHTL) is an integral part of an academic institution, designed to foster a comprehensive and inclusive approach to education. Unlike traditional teaching models that primarily focus on academic instruction, a holistic center emphasizes the development of the whole individual—intellectually, emotionally, socially, and ethically.
At the core of the CHTL is the belief that education is not merely about transferring knowledge but about cultivating skills, values, and attitudes that prepare learners for life. The center acts as a hub for innovative teaching methodologies, interdisciplinary learning, and personalized student engagement. By integrating elements such as experiential learning, collaborative projects, mindfulness, and social responsibility, it creates an environment that nurtures curiosity, creativity, and a love for lifelong learning.
The CHTL supports both educators and students by providing resources such as professional development workshops, technology integration tools, and mentoring programs. It also promotes active learning through state-of-the-art facilities, flexible curriculum designs, and opportunities for real-world application of knowledge.
Mission
To cultivate an inclusive and innovative educational environment that fosters the holistic growth of students and educators, integrating academic excellence with emotional, social, and ethical development to prepare individuals for lifelong success and meaningful contributions to society.
Vision
To become a global leader in holistic education, empowering individuals to achieve their full potential, embrace lifelong learning, and drive positive change in an interconnected and dynamic world
Objectives
To implement teaching and learning approaches that address intellectual, emotional, social, physical, and ethical dimensions of education.
To provide professional development programs and resources that equip educators with innovative teaching methodologies, technology integration, and interdisciplinary strategies.
To instill a culture of curiosity, self-directed learning, and continuous improvement among students and educators.
To integrate mindfulness, emotional intelligence, and well-being programs into the learning process, ensuring a supportive and nurturing environment.
To utilize advanced educational tools and technologies to enhance teaching effectiveness and student engagement.
To promote collaborative projects and interdisciplinary approaches that foster critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills.
To continuously assess the effectiveness of programs and initiatives and refine them to meet evolving educational needs and standards.
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. A. Sujith

PROFILE
Central Computer Centre (CCC)
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. C. Periasamy
CONTACT
Email id:
maincc@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495 228 6852, 0495 228 6854
Mailing Address
Central Computer Centre,National Institute of Technology,calicut 673601
Central Computer Centre (CCC) is the central facility in NIT Calicut which caters for the computing requirements of the whole community of this institution. The Centre has state of the art infrastructure with four fully operational terminals spanning over three floors of the building. Decision Support System (DSS) of the institute also operate from the Centre building. The Centre has 190+ client machines and has a capacity to include 400+ machines. Client systems are of both desktop and workstation genre. Desktops contain HP Prodesk 600 G2, DELL OPTIPLEX 755 and 7010 series which are of adequate performance and workstations contain Fujitsu Celsius W570 power, HP XW4550 and DELL Precision series machines which are capable of more than handling high end production and design level applications.CCC hosts some of the high end servers and a hybrid cluster machine. Servers include DELL PowerEdge T620 which has a dual Hexa-core processor and HP ProLiant Rack server which has a dual Quad-core processor.High performance cluster machine (14 node CPU-GPU cluster system) which has a computational capability of 25 TeraFlops is accessible along the length and the breadth of nitc campus.CCC plans to expand and transform in to a world class facility in the near future with a futuristic outlook by strongly activating the super computer user group activities,and encompassing parallel computing servers,multi processor compute servers and application servers etc..
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. C. Periasamy
CONTACT

Contact no:

Mailing Address

Smart Solar Stove
പ്ലാസ്റ്റിക് മാലിന്യത്തിൽ നിന്ന് വൈദ്യുതി : പി ടു പി പ്ലാൻറ് മന്ത്രി രാജേഷ് സന്ദർശിച്ചു
മന്ത്രി എം. ബി. രാജേഷ് എൻ. ഐ. ടിയിലെ 'പ്ലാസ്റ്റിക് റ്റു പവർ ' പ്ലാൻറ് സന്ദർശിച്ചു
പ്ലാസ്റ്റിക്കിൽനിന്ന് വൈദ്യുതി ; മന്ത്രി പ്ലാൻറ് സന്ദർശിച്ചു
മെഡിക്കൽ അത്യാഹിതങ്ങൾക്കായി ഡ്രോൺ എൻ ഐ ടി സി വിദ്യാർത്ഥികൾക്ക് ദേശീയ അംഗീകാരം
എൻ ഐ ടി സിയുടെ രൂപകൽപ്പന ഒന്നാമത് : മരുന്നുമായി പറക്കും ഡ്രോൺ
മെഡിക്കൽ അത്യാഹിതങ്ങൾക്കായി ഡ്രോണുകൾ: അഭിമാനനേട്ടവുമായി എൻ ഐ ടി വിദ്യാർത്ഥികൾ
മെഡിക്കൽ അത്യാഹിതങ്ങൾക്കായി ഡ്രോണുകൾ വികസിപ്പിച്ചതിന് എൻ ഐ ടി സി വിദ്യാർത്ഥികൾക്ക് ദേശീയ അംഗീകാരം
മെഡിക്കൽ അത്യാഹിത ഡ്രോണുകൾ വികസിപ്പിച്ചു എൻ ഐ ടി വിദ്യാർത്ഥികൾക്ക് ദേശീയ അംഗീകാരം
കണ്ടുപിടുത്തങ്ങളിൽ ദേശീയ നേട്ടവുമായി കാലിക്കറ്റ് എൻ ഐ ടി
ഭാരം ചുമക്കാം ഭയക്കാതെ
പാചകവാതകം വേണ്ട, ചെലവുരഹിത സോളാർ അടുപ്പ് തയ്യാർ
സോളാർ സ്‌റ്റൗ വികസിപ്പിച്ചെടുത്ത് കോഴിക്കോട് എൻ ഐ ടി
അക്ഷരപ്പുര പകരും   ‘സൂര്യ’വെളിച്ചം
എൽ പി ജിക്കു ബദൽ സ്മാർട്ട് സോളാർ സ്റ്റൗവുമായി എൻ ഐ ടി
സ്മാർട്ട് സോളാർ സ്റ്റൗവ് വികസിപ്പിച്ചെടുത്ത് കോഴിക്കോട് നാഷ്ണൽ ഇൻസ്റ്റിറ്റ്യൂട്ട് ഓഫ് ടെക്നോളജി
കരിഞ്ചീരകയെണ്ണ തുരത്തും സൂപ്പർബഗ്ഗുകളെ
പാചകവാതക വില വർദ്ധനവ് തലയ്ക്കടിക്കുമ്പോൾ പ്രതീക്ഷയേകി സ്‌മാർട്ട് സോളാർ സ്റ്റൗ
എൻ ഐ ടി ഗവേഷകർ സ്മാർട്ട് സൗരോർജ അടുപ്പ് വികസിപ്പിച്ചു
ഇനി അടുക്കളയിലും ഫുൾ ചാർജ് , സോളാർ സ്‌റ്റൗവുമായി എൻഐടി

Oct 19, 2024
NIT Calicut Hosts IEDC SUMMIT’24: Empowering Innovation and Entrepreneurship
The 9th edition of the IEDC Summit was hosted by NIT Calicut and Kerala Startup Mission on October 19, 2024 with a mission to empower innovation and entrepreneurship. The IEDC Summit, recognized as one of Asia's largest events, provides a dynamic platform for innovators, entrepreneurs, and students to connect, cooperate, and prosper. This edition of the summit took place on the NITC campus, with the Gurudev Rabindranath Tagore Theatre being the main venue, previously known as OAT (Open Air Theatre).
Dr. Sudheer A P, Chairperson, CIEI-NITC welcomed the gathering, followed by keynote addresses by Sri. Anoop Ambika, CEO of Kerala Startup Mission in the inaugural function which was presided over by  Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NITC. Dr. B.K Das, Director General, Electronics and Communication Systems, DRDO explained the importance of developing indigenous products in his inaugural speech. IEDC Student lead of NITC Mr. Jaseem also was present at the function. Their insightful ideas set the tone for a day full of learning and collaboration.
An engaging panel discussion with Shri. Roshan Kynadi, CEO of  ZuperAPP, emphasized on  fostering innovation within academic institutions. Following which, a fireside chat with Shir. Ishan, CEO of Kadha, and Shri. Mohammed Hisam, CEO of Entri Learning App revealed the speakers’ personal success stories and ideas.
Fireside chats and panel discussions were held in other venues as well. With specialized sessions on topics ranging from AI, Robotics, and Fostering Innovation to Medical Technology, Women's Entrepreneurship, and Finance-Supply chain, the summit catered to a wide array of interests. Thought-provoking discussions on subjects like AI ethics, high-frequency trading, and sustainable technology ensured there was something for everyone.
Intensive workshops on diverse topics took place both online and offline. Workshops on topics like AR/VR/MR, Metaverse, drones, 3D bioprinting, AI model building, cybersecurity, design thinking, freelancing, investment, global civil placements, robotics, and women’s empowerment in technology saw a good number of participation. Organizations such as Unique World Robotics, NITC’s Riglabs, NSRCEL, ENTRI, AICTE, ShelterTech, iNEST, XR Horizon, and many more arranged these workshops that created interactive learning sessions where participants actively engaged in discussions, activities, or hands-on exercises.
An expo of startups was also arranged as part of the summit. 36 startups participated in the expo which attracted a number of visitors.
Around 10,000 students form 502 IEDCs across Kerala participated in the Asia’s largest innovation festival at NIT Calicut on this day. In the valedictory function Sri. Bergin Russel - Assistant Manager, Kerala Startup Mission, Sri. Ashok Kurian - Senior Manager KSUm, Dr. Preethi Manniledam- CEO of TBI NIT Calicut and Sri. Pranav - student lead of IEDC NITC were present.
IEDC Summit’24 has proved to be a grand success, inspiring students, professionals, and innovators to continue pushing the boundaries of entrepreneurship and technological advancements.

Vigilance Corner
Home
The Chief Vigilance Officers act as extended hands of Central Vigilance Commissioner. CVOs provide direction, guidance and supervision over the vigilance efforts of the society and report independently to the CVO of the Department or CVC directly. The vigilance department works for system improvement and simplification and codification of rules and procedures for the smooth functioning of the society towards achievement of its mission
Contact
Chief Vigilance Officer (CVO)
Dr. G. Unnikrishnan

RESEARCH & CONSULTANCY
OVERVIEW
Research & Consultancy Office
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) is actively involved in diverse areas of research and offers consultancy services to industry. NITC entered into the area of active research through projects from DRDO way back in 1980. Currently there is more thrust on interdisciplinary research activities. Sponsored research projects investigated by the faculty and supported by various government and research organizations have helped the Institute in procuring state of the art instruments and equipment necessary for carrying out high quality research. Research projects of academic nature as well as those aimed at solving industrial problems are being pursued.
The Office of the Dean-Research and Consultancy facilitates and coordinates all research and development activities of the Institute. The communication and coordination work, compilation and dissemination of scientific and technical information, registration for patent of products etc. are looked after by this Office
Visit website

NITCAA
Overview
Office bearers
Worldnitcaa DP
NITCAA
About NITCAA
NIT Calicut Alumni Association (
NITCAA
), formerly known as Regional Engineering College Calicut Alumni Association (RECCAA)was established in 2018. This dynamic organization serves as the primary linkage between NIT Calicut and the nearly 40,000 strong alumni community across the globe. The association has 18 chapters – 11 in India and 7 abroad. Faculty members of NIT Calicut are members of NITCAA by default. NITCAA endeavors to commit its members to the advancement of NIT Calicut through programs that instill a sense of belonging to the alma mater and a passion for the life-long relationship between the NITC fraternity and its alumni. NITCAA has spearheaded various institute-building activities and student-centric initiatives such as NITC Alumni Mentoring Program (NITCAMP). While the Digital Platform worldnitcaa.com has been an effective tool for promoting virtual alumni connectivity, the biannual event World NITCAA Meet brings alumni together from all over the world.
Address:
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
NIT Campus P.O 673 601,
Kozhikode, India.
General
Siksha Kaushal (LMS)

Jan 26, 2025
National Institute of Technology Calicut celebrates India’s 76th Republic Day
The National Institute of Technology Calicut proudly celebrated the 76th Republic Day of the nation, honoring the spirit of unity, freedom, and patriotism. The honorable director of the institute, Prof. Prasad Krishna, unfurled the tricolour and offered the Republic Day Message. He urged the youth of the nation to remember India's heritage and culture and the path we have traversed to reach where we are now as a nation.
Captain Shinodh Karthikeyan, Officer in Charge, water survival training facility (WSTF), INS Garuda, was the chief guest of the day and addressed the gathering. He shared his experiences as a member of the nation's defense force and spoke about the Indian Navy's rapid progress and advancements in recent years. Captain Shinodh Karthikeyan is a decorated officer of the Indian Navy, having won commendations for gallantry and devotion to duty. The Republic Day celebrations witnessed a parade by the NCC cadets, security guards, and a march past and procession. A vibrant cultural program followed that brought to life the spirit of our rich and diverse motherland. The celebration was attended by the Registrar, Cdr. (Dr.) Shamasundara M.S, NCC Officer Dr. Chandrashekar Besta, Deans, faculty members and staff, their family members, and students.

CENTRAL FACILITIES
Campus Networking Centre (CNC)
Central Computer Centre (CCC)
Guest House
Engineering Unit
Centre for Career Development
Health Centre
Centre for Computational  Modelling and Simulation
Centre for Materials Characterization (CMC)
Central Research Facilities (CRF)
E-Cube Resources Centre
FACILITY
Engineering Unit
The main functions of the Engineering Unit are to organise all construction, maintenance, electrification, and water supply for the institute campus. The unit prepares proposals based on the needs of different departments for construction of new buildings, roads, electricity requirements, etc. The Engineering Unit handles all of the estimation, presentations to the Building and Works Committee, tendering, and execution. For major buildings, CED handles the structural design, and DAP handles the architectural design. The Engineering Unit is at present headed by a Superintending Engineer, three Executive Engineers (one for Civil and two for Electrical), three Assistant Engineers (two for Civil and one for Electrical). The accounting and auditing of the work are done by a Divisional Accountant. Under this unit, there are other technical staff and supporting staff working in the office as well as in the Pump House, Filter House, and Electrical Maintenance Unit. The total employee strength at present is 40.

Sep 07, 2024
20th Convocation Ceremony held at NIT Calicut
An all-time high of 1979 were awarded degrees
The 20th Convocation ceremony of National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) was held on the campus today. Shri. George Kurian, Hon. Minister of state for Minority Affairs and Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Govt. of India, was the chief guest at the Convocation Ceremony.
“When India accomplishes Viksit Bharat 2047, the young graduates of today will be the leaders of the country, leading industry and economy,” said Shri. George Kurian, Hon. Minister of state for Minority Affairs and Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying. The duty of the government and the professionals will be to support the people and enhance their income by promoting and marketing their efforts, he said adding “Each and every moment of your life, you will be contributing to the country”.
Highlighting the literacy and health achievements of Kerala, he urged the students from Kerala and outside Kerala to be the ambassadors of Kerala, said the Minister. He also urged them to start their business in Kerala and support the state from when they completed their higher education.
By implementing NEP 2020, the Government is envisaging to have a major revamping of our education system and regain the position of our country as Vishwa Guru, which we have lost in between due to various reasons, said Shri. George Kurian.
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Chairperson, BoG in-charge & Director (NITC), presided over the function and declared the convocation ceremony open and presented the report. NITC is the largest NIT in country with over 7800 students, 425 faculty and 330 staff members, said Prof. Prasad Krishna.
Highlighting the placement achievements of NITC, Prof. Prasad Krishna said “196 companies recruited from NITC in 2023-24 academic year, leading to 1,011 placements for graduates”. Undergraduates secured jobs with a highest salary of 50.6 lakhs, while postgraduates had a maximum salary of 39.3 lakhs, he said adding that the average salary for the year was 10.86 lakh.
Shri. Milind Lakkad (distinguished Alumnus of the 1985 batch of Mechanical Engineering Department and Distinguished Alumni Award), Executive Vice President and Chief Human Resources Officer, Tata Consultancy Services was the Guest of Honour. Success is the result of passion combined with consistency and persistence, he said during his address and added that the next most important aspect of a graduate’s journey after passion was Lifelong Learning.
Registrar of the institute Cdr. (Dr) M. S. Shamasundara, Dean (Academic) Prof. A.V. Babu and the Heads of various Departments administered the convocation ceremony.
Graduates:
An all-time high of 1979 graduands were awarded their degrees during the convocation, which included 1169 B.Tech., 57 B.Arch., 442 M.Tech., 23 M.Plan., 56 MCA, 44 MBA, 94 M.Sc., and 94 PhD degrees.
Gold Medalists and Best Outgoing Students
Ms. A. Anjana from the Civil Engineering branch who secured the highest CGPA (9.88/10) among all the outgoing UG students of the Institute was awarded the *‘Er. M L Bapna Gold Medal’ and a cash prize of Rs.11,111/-. She also bagged the ‘Prof. Allesu Kanjirathinkal Memorial Award’* and Cash prize of Rs.10,000/- for the topper among all UG programmes.
Among the PG students, Mr. Syed Abdul Khader Jeelan Arafath from the M.Tech Electrical Engineering in Power Systems bagged the *‘Er. M L Bapna Gold Medal’* and a cash prize of Rs.11,111/- with a CGPA of 9.9 out of 10.
Mr. Alias George from the Electronics and Communication Engineering branch secured the *Canara Bank sponsored Outstanding Achiever Award* and cash prize of Rs.25,000/-. He was also awarded with the *Vikram Sarabhai Ever Rolling Trophy* sponsored by the first batch of REC Calicut for the Best Outgoing UG Student.
Toppers
Toppers of the 10 B. Tech, B. Arch, 24 M. Tech, M. Plan, 3 M.Sc., MCA and MBA programmes of the Institute were also awarded Gold Medals for their outstanding performance in their respective specializations.

May 16, 2025
NIT Calicut Signs MoU with TU Berlin to Foster Global Academic Collaboration
In a landmark initiative to strengthen international academic cooperation, the National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Technische Universität Berlin (TU Berlin) on 12 may 2025. The MoU was formalized at TU Berlin in the presence of faculty members and delegates from both institutions.
This agreement aims to promote academic exchange, joint research initiatives, and collaboration in key domains such as international urbanism, sustainable urban development, and architectural design. It represents a strategic step towards enabling impactful interdisciplinary projects and enhancing student and faculty mobility between India and Germany.
The MoU signing took place during the official NITC Delegation to Europe, led by Prof. (Dr.) Prasad Krishna, Director, NIT Calicut. The delegation also included:
Prof. (Dr.) Ravi Varma Mundakkara Kovilakam, Dean (International Affairs and Corporate Relations),Dr. Maneesh Chandran, Associate Dean (International Relations) and Ms. Kavitha Gajanur Rudrappa, Advisor (International Relations).
TU Berlin was represented by Prof. Dr. Anke Hagemann, a distinguished expert in urban design and international planning strategies. Dr. Chithra K from NITC’s Department of Architecture served as the faculty lead for this initiative and was instrumental in facilitating the collaboration.
This MoU reaffirms the mutual commitment of both institutions to address global urban challenges through collaborative research and academic exchange. It also aligns with NIT Calicut’s vision of emerging as a leading global center for academic and research excellence.

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
May 21, 2025
Foundation Stone Laid for Sushruta Swasthya Kendram at NIT Calicut
NIT Calicut has taken a significant step forward in promoting holistic well-being with the foundation stone laying of the Sushruta Swasthya Kendram, a modern wellness centre designed to enhance the physical and mental health of the campus community. This initiative aligns with the institute's broader mission of integrating well-being with academic excellence.
The project is a collaborative endeavor between NIT Calicut and the NIT Calicut Alumni Association (NITCAA), reflecting the power of alumni engagement in shaping a healthier future for students, faculty, and staff.
The ceremony commenced with a Welcome Address by Prof. Ravi Varma M. K., Dean IACR, who outlined the vision and objectives of the wellness centre. The event's highlight was the unveiling of the foundation plaque by Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director NIT Calicut, followed by the ceremonial laying of the foundation stone, accompanied by distinguished guests.
In his address, Prof. Prasad Krishna emphasized the importance of the centre, describing it as a vital step toward fostering a culture of wellness across campus.
Mr. Thankachan Thomas V, President of NITCAA (2024–26), was honored for his leadership and pivotal role in driving the project forward, particularly in rallying alumni support and transforming vision into action.
Representing the 1999 Batch of NIT Calicut Dr. Saleel Ismail, Associate Professor,NITC acknowledged the generous contributions of his batchmates, underscoring the lasting impact and commitment of alumni toward the institute's development.
The event concluded with a Vote of Thanks by Mr. Ranganathan Ramani, Secretary of NITCAA who expressed heartfelt gratitude to all contributors and well-wishers for their steadfast support in making this wellness centre a reality.

PROFILE
Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. R. Sridharan
CONTACT
Email id:
ciks@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 495 2286406 / 9495788136
Mailing Address
Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems
National Institute of Technology Calicut
NIT Campus P.O., Kozhikode 673601
In line with the National Education Policy 2020, NITC has established the Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems (CIKS, Bharatheeya Gyana Parampara Kendram) to promote education, training and research in traditional Indian Knowledge Systems (Science, Mathematics, Economics, Astronomy, Astrology).
Vision
Intellectuals empowered with Indian Knowledge Systems to address technological challenges.
Mision
Enable students, faculty and staff to engage in education and research on Indian Knowledge Systems for developing appropriate technologies/practices and products for sustainable growth.
Brochure
Second International Conference on Bhartiya Traditional Knowledge System during July 18-19, 2025
The Centre for Sanskrit Learning (CSL), NIT Calicut is conducting the One-Year Certificate Course of the Central Sanskrit University (CSU), New Delhi from 9th September 2024 onwards
Lecture on “
Science and Spirituality
” by
Sri. Chanchalapathi Dasa
, Vice Chairman and Co-Mentor, Global Hare Krishna Movement, Senior Vice President of the International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON), Bangalore, and Vice Chairman of the Akshaya Patra Foundation, Bangalore on 1
st
September, 2024 from 4.15 p.m. to 5.30 p.m. in Bhasakara Hall, NIT Calicut.
Report on the Workshop on “Innovation and Adaptability: Mahabharata’s Timeless Relevance in a Rapidly Changing Business Landscape”, 16th March 2024.
IKS Centre Project Sanctioned by the IKS Division of Ministry of Education @ AICTE
Title of the Project
I
ndian Knowledge Systems Approach to Deep Ecology: Integrating Sacred Geography and Sustainability
Investigators
Dr. Anjana Bhagyanathan, Asst. Professor, Department of Architecture and Planning, NITC
Dr. Ramesh Krishnamurthy, WII
Dr.A.Srivatsan, CEPT
Dr.Chithra K, Associate Professor, Department of Architecture and Planning, NITC,
Dr.Deepak Dhayanithy, IIMK
Dr.Gauri Mahulikar, Chinmaya International Foundation
Dr.M D Subashchandran, IISc. Bangalore
Year of Starting
December 2022
Amount
Rs. 25 Lakhs
Award of Post-Doctoral Fellowship by the Indian Council of Social Science Research (ICSSR)
Post-Doc Scholar: Dr. Nidhi Yadav
Title of the Research Theme: Role of Indian Knowledge System in National Education Policy 2020 and Sustainable Development Goals
Supervisor: Dr. R. Sridharan, Professor (HAG), Mechanical Engineering Department and Chairperson – Centre for Indian Knowledge Systems
Fellowship Duration: Two years w.e.f 11-12-2023
Past Event
Workshop on “Innovation and Adaptability: Mahabharata’s Timeless Relevance in a Rapidly Changing Business Landscape”, 16th March 2024.
Short Term Training Programme on Indian Knowledge Systems in Environmental Conservation & Sustainable Development (IKSECSD-2024), Organised by the Centre For Indian Knowledge Systems, National Institute Of Technology Calicut, January 30 - February 3, 2024.
Brochure and Programme Schedule
30th Swadeshi Science Congress will be held in NITC during 25-27 May 2023. -

May 20, 2025
NIT Calicut Delegation Explores Strategic Academic Collaboration at NTNU, Norway
A high-level delegation from the National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC), led by Director Prof. Prasad Krishna, visited the Norwegian University of Science and Technology (NTNU) on 15 May 2025 to advance Indo-Norwegian cooperation in education, research, and innovation.
The visit reaffirmed a shared institutional commitment to fostering international academic partnerships and featured several key engagements:
Presentation on Information Systems and Architecture (ISA): NTNU faculty highlighted interdisciplinary approaches to systems design and digital transformation, underlining the university’s research strengths in technology and computing.
Strategic Collaboration Dialogue: Discussions focused on expanding joint research initiatives, launching dual-degree postgraduate programs, and facilitating undergraduate exchange pathways between the two institutions.
Review of Bilateral Research Projects: The delegation reviewed the ongoing India–Norway collaborative research initiative co-led by Dr. Kiran Raja (NTNU) and Dr. Sudhish George (NITC), emphasizing sustained cooperation in emerging technologies.
Internationalization Roundtable: Both teams engaged in an open exchange on curriculum integration and institutional strategies for promoting academic mobility and cross-cultural engagement.
This visit marks a significant milestone in strengthening academic relations between India and Norway. It sets the foundation for long-term collaboration in interdisciplinary research, academic exchange, and the co-creation of global learning opportunities for students and faculty.
NIT Calicut continues to pursue strategic international partnerships in alignment with its vision of global excellence in science, technology, and education.

May 26, 2025
NIT Calicut Conducts Life-Saving Mock Drill Under ‘Rashtra Pratham’ Campaign
In a powerful display of emergency preparedness,
NIT Calicut
hosted a large-scale
Rescue and Evacuation Mock Drill
as part of the nationwide
‘Rashtra Pratham’ (Nation First)
campaign. This initiative, driven by the
Ministry of Education
and
Ministry of Home Affairs
, aims to instill civil preparedness and promote national unity across institutions.
Emergency in Action: Learning Through Simulation
At exactly
3:30 PM
, the campus came alive with the sound of sirens and the swift movement of coordinated rescue teams. Students, faculty, and staff gathered to witness real-time simulations of disaster response—including fire emergencies, building evacuations, and safety protocol enactments.
The event was held in collaboration with the
Kerala Fire and Rescue Services
and the
Civil Defense Team
, ensuring professional execution and authenticity in every aspect of the drill.
Leadership with Purpose
The event was formally inaugurated by
Prof. Prasad Krishna
, Director NIT Calicut.
“Preparedness is key. We must empower every member of our community to act swiftly and responsibly during a crisis,”
he emphasized during his opening remarks, highlighting the institute’s proactive approach to safety and resilience.
Expert Insights for Real-Life Emergencies
Attendees also benefited from a hands-on session by
Abdul Gafoor M
, Station Officer from Mukkam. His session covered critical disaster response techniques and emphasized life-saving actions during fires, earthquakes, and other emergencies. Practical, easy-to-remember tips gave the community actionable knowledge to carry forward.
Building a Campus-Wide Safety Force
In a significant step forward, Prof. Prasad Krishna announced the upcoming formation of an
in-house disaster management team
at NIT Calicut.
“Our goal is to ensure that help is always just a few steps away,” he stated.
The team, to include trained representatives from each department, will receive professional training from the Kerala Fire Force.
A Commitment to Safety and Solidarity
Through this mock drill,
NIT Calicut
not only reaffirmed its commitment to campus safety but also echoed the values of
unity, vigilance, and civic responsibility
that the Rashtra Pratham campaign represents.

PROFILE
Advanced Manufacturing Centre
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. Jose Mathew
CONTACT
Website:
https://www.amc.nitc.ac.in/
Email id:
josmat@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 9447416639
Mailing Address
Dr. Jose Mathew
Professor (HAG)
Department of Mechanical Engineering
National Institute of Technology Calicut
NIT Campus P.O., Kerala - 673 601, India
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) is one of the 31 institutions of national importance governed by the NIT Act 2007 and is fully funded by the Government of India. Originally established in 1961 as a Regional Engineering College (REC), it was transformed into a National Institute of Technology in the year 2002. The institute offers bachelors, masters and doctoral degree programme in Engineering, Science, Technology and Management. With its proactive collaborations with a multitude of research organizations, academic institutions and industries, the institute has set a new style for its functioning under the NIT regime. The Institute is presently offering 11 UG programme and thirty PG programme along with Ph.D. programme in various fields of Engineering, Science Technology and Management;
Department of Mechanical Engineering is the largest and one of the oldest departments in the Institute. It offers two undergraduate and six postgraduate programmes apart from Ph.D. programmes in diverse specializations. It also offers a number of short term/continuing education programmes. It is a DST- FIST sponsored department and recently DST has sanctioned Rs 251 lakhs under FIST scheme to set up a Centre for Precision Measurements and Nanomechanical Testing
Visit Website

RESEARCH & CONSULTANCY UNITS
Institutional Biosafety Committee (IBSC)
In compliance with Rules 1989, an Institutional Biosafety Committee (IBSC) is to be constituted by every organisation engaged in research, use & application activities related to GE organisms (organisms include microorganisms, animals, plants, arthropods, aquatic animals, etc.) and hazardous microorganisms ( "microorganisms" shall include all the bacteria, viruses, fungi, mycoplasma, cells lines, algae, protodones and nematotes). IBSC is the nodal agency within an organization for implementation of the biosafety regulatory framework. DBT/RCGM has been entrusted with registration and monitoring of IBSCs.
Visit IBSC website

National Institute of Technology Calicut,

Multidisciplinary Centres
Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Management Studies
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2008
Head of the Department:
Dr. Nithya M.
CONTACT
Email id:
hoddms@nitc.ac.in, dmsoffice@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2286075/77, 04952993077
Mailing Address
dmsoffice@nitc.ac.in
Department of Management Studies, National Institute of Technology Calicut, started in 2008 to intertwine technical skills and managerial proficiency. The department of Management Studies endeavours to develop the analytical and organisational skills required for a professional manager and to enrich them with adequate background and universal values to enable them to play an influential role as managers for the benefit of society.
The learning programmes at the department of Management Studies, National Institute of Technology Calicut, are designed to satisfy the demands of the corporate world, not discounting the importance of entrepreneurship. It will undoubtedly enable young, dynamic professionals to re-assess their credentials, broaden their skills, learn new techniques and secure a wider choice of careers.
The curriculum is designed to develop professional managers who adapt to the fast-changing environment while maintaining sight of human values and ethos.The curriculum emphasizes enhancing the ability of the students to take an integrated view of managerial situations and strengthen their capabilities through applying professional management principles under varying situations.
The learning at the department of Management Studies focuses on International-perspective, Learner-centric approach, Industry relevance, Value-based. Entrepreneurship-oriented education system (ILIVE), all imparted with sustainability at the core.
Vision
Internationally acclaimed professional competency of highest calibre.
Mission
To equip, train and mould students into globally competent personnel with sound footing on basic managerial principles, skills, research capabilities, along with exemplary professional conduct to analyze, evaluate and execute effective decisions for the progress of enterprises and society, adapting to dynamic economic environments towards robust governance with the highest ethical values.

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
NITC continues stellar placement record
Total placements cross 1000 for the second consecutive year
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) has achieved the milestone of 1000+ placements for the second consecutive year. NITC students secured placements in firms like D. E. Shaw, Salesforce, Google, Goldman Sachs, TI, Qualcomm, Oracle, Cisco, Atlassian, SAP Labs, Micron, Marvell, Samsung, Fastenal, L&T, IBM, TCS, Caterpillar, HCL, Bosch, Infosys, Wipro, Samsung, Maruti Suzuki and Tata Motors among a total of 188 companies who visited the campus so far for recruitment this year. The campus has also seen a strong positive response from public sector placements this year.
The highest offer made this year is Rs. 47 Lakhs Per Annum and the average offer for Computer Science and Engineering students has increased to Rs. 22.03 Lakhs from Rs. 20.54 Lakhs last year. Average offer to ECE students has seen an increase to Rs. 19.29 Lakhs from Rs. 15.34 Lakhs at the same time last year. The overall average this year has risen to Rs. 14.29 Lakhs from Rs. 12.91 Lakhs at the same time last year.
This achievement is a testament to the credentials of the student community and the efforts of the institute in developing innovative teaching methods and career-readiness training programs for the students. Commitment to high quality education and research and efforts to provide opportunities for students to excel in their chosen field of study has had a significant impact in achieving this goal.
In line with the new education policy, the institute has a strong focus on internships at industry and research organisations. The new Centre for Industry-Institute Relations, along with our strong 35000+ alumni network, has ensured strong relationships with both public sector and private industry and other research organisations, both within and outside the country.
NIT Calicut has been incorporating novel teaching methods to make learning more interactive and effective. Consistent efforts were put to improve faculty-student ratio with recruitment of around 200 new faculty with academic and industry experience in India and abroad. The institute introduced various initiatives, curricular and co-curricular activities like flipped classrooms, lectures and interaction with Industry experts through co-teaching, peer-to-peer learning, gamification, and experiential learning to make learning more engaging and exciting for students.
The Centre for Career Development (CCD-NITC) has been providing career-readiness training programs to students to make them industry-ready. The centre has been conducting various training sessions, workshops, coding programs, hackathons and CV clinics to help students develop the necessary skills to excel in their chosen fields. The centre's focus on soft skills, communication, and personality development has helped students to become confident and articulate.
Commenting on this achievement, Dr. Praveen Sankaran, the Chairperson of CCD, said, "We are thrilled to have achieved this milestone and are proud of the achievements of our students. Our faculty, CCD-NITC team and staff have been working tirelessly to ensure that our students get the best opportunities, and this achievement is a testament to their efforts. The recent NBA 6 year full accreditation of our five undergraduate programmes augurs well for the future career prospects of our students".

NIT-C records over 1,000 placements for second consecutive year
- The Hindu

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
Sep 01, 2024
NITC celebrated the 64th Foundation Day
National Institute of Technology Calicut celebrated the 64th Foundation Day honouring employees, alumni, and retired employees also recalling the various achievements of the institute, students, staff, faculty and alumni over the past year. Sri Chanchalapathi Dasa, Vice Chairman and Co-Mentor of the Global Hare Krishna Movement and Senior Vice President of ISKCON Bangalore was the Chief Guest on the occasion.
Sri Chanchalapathi Dasa delivered the Dr. M. V. Kesava Rao Foundation Day Memorial Lecture in memory of the institute's founding principal. In his address, he highlighted the stress experienced by youngsters due to excessive exposure to too much information, pointing out the cases of suicide among highly talented youngsters. He also stressed the need to follow India's tradition to evade stress.
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut, presided over the programme and delivered the State of the Institute Address. NITC is the largest NIT in India with over 7500 students, 425 faculty members and over 300 non-teaching staff, he added that the institute recruited over 100 faculty and over 200 non-faculty in the past year.
Shri Ravi Raghavan, an alumnus of the 1983-88 batch of the Institute and Managing Director of Bharat Fritz Werner Ltd (BFW), was the guest of honour. In his address, he urged the students to master their knowledge in one vertical and explore innovations and knowledge in multidisciplinary areas to face the challenges in the modern world.
Cdr. Dr. Shamasundara M.S., Registrar of NITC; Dr. G. K. Rajanikant, General Convener of the Programme and Chairperson of C-PRIME and Dr. M. K. Ravi Varma, General Convener of the programme and Dean (International, Alumni and Corporate Relations) also spoke at the event.  Upholding the theme of the foundation Day ‘Sustainability with Technology’ the guests also planted tree saplings on the South Campus of the Institute.
Awards and Recognition:
The Institute also honoured 8 alumni of the Institute by offering them the Distinguished Alumni Award. The winners are Shri J A Kamalakar, Director ISRO / Honorary Adviser ISRO [Technology Innovation Excellence], Shri Roshan V. Joseph, A. Russell Chandler III Chair Professor, Georgia Institute of Technology [Academic Excellence], Shri Milind Lakkad, Executive Vice President & CHRO, Tata Consultancy Services [Managerial Excellence], Shri Subba Rao Pavuluri, Chairman & Managing Director, Ananth Technologies [Entrepreneurial Excellence], Shri Hari Shankar, IPS Officer, SP Cyber Operations, Kerala Police [Excellence in Public Services and Other Walks of Life], Smt. Usha P Verma, Outstanding Scientist & Associate Director, ASL, DRDO [Excellence in Institutional Development and Alma Mater Services], Shri Saurabh Sinha, Founder & Chairman, EMIDS [Excellence in Institutional Development and Alma Mater Services] and Shri Nidhin Valsan, IPS Officer, DCP, Delhi Police [Emerging Alumni Leader Award].
The institute honoured 13 retired faculty and non-faculty members, aged above 85, at the event. Besides, the past presidents of NITCAA (NITC Alumni Association), and employees who have completed 25 years of service at NITC were honoured.
The institute also presented five proficiency awards to the wards of NITC employees who secured high marks in class 10 and 12 exams.
The Union Bank of India Institute Research and Development Award 2024 for researchers for lifetime, Mid and Early Career achievements. The winners are Dr. Santosh G. Thampi, Professor of Civil Engineering; Dr. Kumaravel S, Associate Professor of Electrical Engineering; and Dr. Noel Jacob Kaleekal.
The Bank of Baroda Young Investigator Award was presented to Dr. Basil Kuriachan from the Mechanical Engineering Department.
The Institute also conferred the State Bank of India Best Teacher award in Professor, Associate Professor and Assistant Professor Category up on Prof. G Unnikrishnan, Chemistry Department; Dr. Sudheer A. P., Mechanical Engineer; Dr. Yogeshwar Vijaykumar Navandar, Civil Engineering.
Skylight Expo 2024
An exhibition of projects and innovations by students of different institute departments was on display as part of the Foundation Day Celebration. The Exhibition will remain open for school-college students on Monday.
