Nov 26, 2024
NIT Calicut Commemorates Constitution Day with Enthusiasm and Insight
The National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) Celebrated
Constitution Day
with a series of inspiring events that honor the spirit and values of the Indian Constitution. The program took place on 26 November 2024, fostering awareness about the Constitution's importance among students, faculty, and staff.
The event began with a
Welcome Address
delivered by
Prof. Satyananda Panda
, marking the start of a day dedicated to reflection and learning.
Prof. Priya Chandran
, Director-In-Charge & Dean of Planning and Development delivered the presidential address followed by ceremonial release of the
Preamble of the Constitution
.
A key highlight of the event is the guest lecture by
Mr. Nidhin Jose
, who presented an engaging session titled,
“Fundamental Rights and the Constitution of India.”
This insightful talk shed light on the significance and implications of fundamental rights as enshrined in the Constitution. The celebrations honored student achievements through a
Prize Distribution Ceremony
, recognizing winners of various competitions, including
Essay Writing
,
Poster Design
, and
English Extempore
. The event concluded with a Vote of Thanks proposed by Mr. Ramesh S,(AR Estate) expressing gratitude to all participants, contributors, and organizers.

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record

May 26, 2025
Leadership training by Centre for Holistic Teaching and Learning
The Centre for Holistic Teaching and Learning (CHTL) organized a one-day leadership training workshop for the young faculty members of our institute on 14th May 2025. The resource persons for the workshop were selected NITC faculty members who had previously attended the National Futuristic Leadership Programme (NFLP).
The training was led by Dr. V. Sivasubramanian, Dr. Ashutosh Mishra, Dr. Vrinda Varma, Dr. Nitya M., Dr. Jay Prakash, Dr. Fathima Fasmin, Dr. Lintu Rajan, and Dr. Manju Mahipalan. A total of 38 participants attended the workshop.
The program was coordinated by Dr. A. Sujith (Chairperson, CHTL) and Prof. T.K. Sureshbabu (Professor, Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences).

DEPARTMENTS
NIT Calicut has a total of 15 departments in addition to the various Multidisciplinary Centres and Thematic Centres. These various entities continuously strive hard independently and in cohesion for realizing the vision of the Institute, 'International standing of the highest calibre'.

PROFILE
Materials Science and Engineering
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2019
Head of the Department:
Dr. Hanas T
CONTACT
Email id:
hodsmse@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91 495 228 6501
Mailing Address
Head of the department, Department of Materials Science & Engineering
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
Kozhikode, Kerala, INDIA, 673 601
The Department of Materials Science and Engineering ( Formerly School of Nano Science and Technology ) at National Institute of Technology Calicut offers programs at the Bachelors, Master’s and Doctoral levels, in various academic streams related to Materials Science and Engineering . The department also houses sponsored research projects from various funding agencies. The faculty, students and research scholars are involved in dedicated learning and research to explore, understand and improve materials and processes from a fundamental perspective. They pursue academic research leading to the mission to introduce new functionalities into existing materials, and to tailor-make materials with exceptional qualities, which are the two major goals of Materials Science and Engineering . The varied interests of the department include study of nanoscale physical phenomena, materials for sustainable energy solutions, nanodevices, nanomaterials and composites, biosensors, targeted drug delivery systems, bio-implants, experimental research in nanoscale flow and heat transfer, optical measurements, combustion and nanoparticle fuel additives, synthesis of nanomaterials including carbon nanotubes, and discrete computational studies on nanoscale systems using molecular dynamics simulations. A centre for characterization and Electron Microscopy also exists in the department. The department conducts four-semester Master of Technology program in Nanotechnology . A bachelor of technology program in Materials Science and Engineering will be launched from 2019 admission onward.
Department Brochure
Vision
‘Pioneering Innovations with Passion for Knowledge’
Mission
To develop high quality technical personnel with a sound footing on basic scientific and engineering principles, innovative research capabilities, exemplary professional conduct and adherence to ethical values, who undertake a leadership role in applying Materials Science and Engineering and Nanoscience and Nanotechnology for the progress of mankind
Notice Board
Syllabus for National Level PhD Entrance Examination MSED-July 2025

May 19, 2024
97th Avenue by Alumni Opened at NITC
17th of May 2024 was a memorable day for NITCAA with the inauguration and handing over ceremonies of 2 projects from Alumni to NIT Calicut.
97th Avenue, envisaged by the 1997 batch, and funded by the 1997 batch in a collective manner and Shri. Dharmendra Kumar Agrawal (1971 batch) was inaugurated by Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director NIT Calicut on 17th May by unveiling the plaque. Mr. Mani James, Mr. Deepak Somarajan, Mrs. Zarina, Mr. Vinod C, Mrs. Rathnaveni Sunil, Mr. Paritosh Lal, Mr. Praveen S, Mrs. Smitha Vijayaraghavan, and Mr. Anand V S from the class of 1997 represented the entire batch for the inauguration. Mr. Prakash Shetty (President, NITCAA), Mr. Sunil Kumar R (Secretary, NITCAA), Mr. Thankachan Thomas (Vice President, NITCAA), Dr. Mohammed Firoz C (Treasurer, NITCAA), Mr. Sadeesh P (NITCAA Calicut Chapter President), Mr. Santhosh Melekalathil (NITCAA Cochin Chapter Secretary), and Mr. Charley J Thomas represented the Governing Council of NITCAA. The function was attended by the Director, Registrar, Deans, HODS, Alumni Faculty of the Institute, staff, and students. The welcome address was given by Mr. Prakash Shetty. He mentioned that the "Eye", which is an integral part of the 97th Avenue, will act as the eye of the Alumni watching the happenings in the Institute. Prof. Prasad Krishna addressed the gathering and thanked NITCAA for realizing this project. He remarked that this project will inspire other batches to contribute to the alma mater as well. This was followed by an address by Dr. M K Ravi Varma, Dean International, Alumni and Corporate Relations. Mr. Sadeesh P who volunteered to monitor the project based on the request from NITCAA was specially mentioned in his address. The event concluded with a vote of thanks that was delivered by Shri. Mani James, representative of the 97th batch and Secretary, NITCAA Chennai Chapter.
This was followed by the handing over ceremony of a dental care facility that was initiated by Shri. Thankachan Thomas, Vice President of NITCAA and Senior Executive Vice President of the Prestige Group, in association with the Annoor Dental College and Hospital in Muvattupuzha. The ceremonial handing over of the dental care facility from NITCAA to NITC was carried out at the facility that was set for dental care in the Student Amenity Centre situated on the Campus.
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut, and the members of NITCAA after the inauguration of 97th Avenue.
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut receiving the dental facility Shri. Thankachan Thomas, Vice President of NITCAA

The Student Grievance Redressal Cell
The Student Grievance Redressal Cell (SGRC) at the National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) addresses complaints from students. It evaluates each complaint and provides a suitable resolution. The main objectives of SGRC are:
To maintain the institute's dignity by fostering a peaceful campus environment.
To promote positive relationships among students and between students and teachers.
To encourage students to freely and openly voice their concerns about academic, non-academic, resource-related, and personal issues without fear of retaliation.
How to File a Grievance
Who Can File:
Any student with a genuine grievance related to academic or non-academic issues within the campus.
Where to File:
Approach the Dean Student’s Welfare Office (DSWO) in person.
How to File:
Submit a written complaint in the prescribed format, which should be recommended and forwarded by your Faculty Advisor (FA) and the Head of the Department (HOD).
What Happens Next:
The received grievances are sent to the appropriate committees for further action based on the severity of the issue.
The SGRC is here to ensure a supportive and harmonious environment for all students at NITC.
Student Grievance Complaint form

Multidisciplinary Centres
Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences
OVERVIEW
Head of the Department:
Dr. Sunitha S.
CONTACT
Email id:
hasoffice@nitc.ac.in, hodhas@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
HAS office: 0495-2286075, HOD office: 0495-2286795
Mailing Address
The Head of the Department, Department of Humanities,Arts & Social Sciences, National Institute of Technology Calicut,NITC P.O,Kozhikode-673601, Kerala,India. Fax: +91-495-2287201
The Department of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences (HAS) was established in 2023. Earlier, the faculty was associated with the School of Management Studies, since its inception in 2008. However, recognizing the importance of humanities, arts and social sciences to technical sciences, HAS was constituted as a separate department. The integration of HAS into technical education is especially valuable in the complex environments that we live in today. Responding to today’s global, social, cultural and economic challenges requires specialized knowledge of people, societies, cultures and financial environments. By engaging with diverse disciplines, graduates gain a broader perspective and develop the intellectual flexibility to adjust to emerging technologies and adapt to new challenges.
In a world increasingly driven by technology and innovation, the significance of technical education is undeniable. Engaging with humanities, arts, and social sciences, however, helps foster critical thinking and creative problem-  solving skills, vital for engineers, technologists, and scientists to approach issues from diverse perspectives. The ability to analyze complex texts, dissect historical events, and delve into philosophical inquiries equips graduates with the tools to think critically, evaluate information, and formulate effective solutions.
HAS offers courses in Economics and English Studies to the undergraduate students of NITC in addition to offering a diverse and vibrant PhD programme across a variety of disciplines including, but not limited to Behavioural Economics, Gender Economics, Micro Economics, Early Childhood Education, Queer Studies, Translation Studies, Culture Studies, Film Studies Memory Studies and English Studies. HAS also offers elective courses in Communication skills, New Literatures, Critical Studies, Finance and Economics.

Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Industry - Institutional Relations (CIIR)
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2022
Chairperson:
Dr. Murali K P
CONTACT
Email id:
ciir@nitc.ac.in
Mailing Address
Chairperson
Dr. Murali K P, 
National Institute of Technology Calicut, 
Kozhikode Kerala INDIA 673 601
Ongoing interaction between the Industry and Institute is an essential one. We, as an institution, put great emphasis on Industry-Institute Interaction activities. We put in every effort required to provide the industry with highly qualified and skilled fresh engineers. To strengthen the relationship between industry and our institute, the Center for Industry Institution Relationship (CIIR) was established in the year 2022 and led by a leader with over 30+ years of R&D experience.
CIIR encourages industry experts to continuously partake in curriculum design and career development programs which play a significant role in honing the students' skill set towards the industry's expectations. Towards upscaling the academia in the Global knowledge index, ample industrial exposure is provided to the faculty members to enhance the Teaching Learning Process and enable the Institution to produce quality students. It provides an excellent platform for both the students and faculty members to be aware of industry expectations from the graduates. CIIR serves as a podium to highlight the best practices, the latest technologies from an industry standpoint, and their implementation.
Vision
To enable NITC to produce industry ready graduates and relevant world class research addressing real problems.
Mission
To improve the curriculum, research and teaching process in NITC by collaborating with industry and research institutes so as to produce engineering/science/management graduates who are industry ready and have leadership qualities and are willing to develop sustainable solutions for real/practical issues
Objectives
To establish a centre of excellence by industry/ corporate to provide real-time exposure to technologies, industrial training, campus recruitment opportunities, and internships
To have a bonding between Industry – Institute relationships
Visits of industry executives and practicing engineers to the Institute for seeing research work and laboratories, discussions, and delivering lectures on industrial practices, trends, and experiences.
Joint Research Work and Consultancy by Faculty, Students, and Experts from Industries
To Involve Students in Industrial Projects
Workshops, conferences, and symposia with joint participation of the faculty, students, and the industries
To enhance the student’s interest in entrepreneurship and business strategies
To work as a platform to mold the students according to the requirements of the corporate world as well as Research & Development concern
To Organize In-Plant Training for Students
To assist the department to establish R&D Laboratories sponsored by industries
To establish convergence with industrial and research organizations from various fields through MOUs as a form of interaction.
OVERVIEW

INTERNAL COMPLAINTS COMMITTEE
Overview
People
Message from Internal Complaints Committee (ICC), NIT Calicut
Vishaka Guidelines against Sexual Harassment at workplace
Sexual Harassment Act 2013
UGC (Prevention, prohibition and redressal of sexual harassment of women employees and students in higher educational institutions) Regulations, 2015
Awareness programmes conducted
Students Conduct and Disciplinary Code 2022
Events Conducted
INTERNAL COMPLAINTS COMMITTEE
Objectives of the Committee
As per the Section 4 of the sexual Harassment of Women at workplace (Prevention,Prohibition & Redressal) Act 2013:
To give awareness about matters related to prevention, prohibition and redressal of issues of sexual harassment at workplace
To ensure impartial, transparent enquiry of specific and valid cases presented before the Committee for the benefit and welfare of the NITC community.

PROFILE
Centre for Clean Energy and Circular Economy
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Shaija A.
CONTACT
Mailing Address
Centre for Clean Energy and Circular Economy (C-CECE) is a Multi-disciplinary Centre in NIT Calicut, that aims to converge the activities related to clean energy and circular economy, aligning closely with the related national goals and missions of our country. The Centre aspires in bringing together the capabilities various members of the Institute working in the area of clean energy, enhancing inter-department collaborations within the institute and with other academic/research institutions, NGOs, and government/private organizations.
Vision
To be a leading global centre in the field of clean energy and circular economy aligning with the Sustainable Development Goals.
Mission
To develop effective and affordable solutions for clean and sustainable energy, and circular economy.
To develop and commercialize technologies for clean water and sanitation for good health and well-being.
To develop technologies and solutions for sustainable/clean smart cities and communities and for climate resilience.
Objectives
Develop unique and translational research facilities, and carry out advanced research in the areas of clean energy and sustainable technologies aiding to circular economy.
Create opportunities and support multi-disciplinary research and teaching activities on clean energy systems.
Create a framework for obtaining grants from Government/Private funding agencies and alumni.
Establish research collaborations with industries/alumni/academic and research institutions working in the energy domain and circular economy, and to utilise their expertise for the development of the Centre.
Develop appropriate technologies for waste-to-energy, smart sustainable cities, clean air and water in collaboration with local stakeholders (e.g. farmers, civic bodies).
Offer educational programmes like short term course/training/internships/ workshop/seminars/Masters/PhD programmes in clean and sustainable energy and circular economy.
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Shaija A.
CONTACT
Mailing Address
Address:
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
NIT Campus P.O 673 601,
Kozhikode, India.
General
Siksha Kaushal (LMS)

Faculty/Staff Achievements - Awards
Dr. Chiranjit Ray Assistant Professor (Grade I), Department of Mathematics was awarded A.K. Agarwal Award 2024 for his research paper entitled “Distribution and Divisibility of the Fourier Coefficients of Certain Hauptmoduln” at 90th Annual Conference of Indian Mathematical Society, An International Meet, held at MIT-WPU, Pune
An Indian Patent (555685) on “A Novel Corrugated Heat Sink with Hybrid Co-Filler Interfaced Microcapsules for Effective Thermal Management in Electronic Devices“ has been granted to Dr. Sudev Das, Ms. Aishwarya V of Department of Chemical Engineering
An Indian Patent (555020) on “An invention entitled an effervescent tablet formation by a 3D printing process and method of preparation“ has been granted to Dr. Baiju G. of Department of Bioscience and Engineering, Co-Inventors: Mr. Kalathum Padikkal Arunraj, Mr. Abey Joseph
Trade Mark PP (Short form of "Plastic to Power") has been registered in the names of Prof. Lisa Sreejith and NIT Calicut
Dr. Sameer, Professor, Electronics and Communication Engineering Department at NITC, has been elected as the IEEE Region-10 Director-Elect for the term 2025-26
SAMUDRA: AI CoE Project on Sustainable cities
Patented work on Tubular Reactor for Phenolic Resole Resin
Dr. M. Abdul Akbar, Assistant Professor, CED (Co-PI), received the SERB-CRG project worth 21 lakhs.
Dr. Indumathi Sathisaran (Assistant Professor BSED) awarded RSC Researcher Collaborations Grant by the Royal Society of Chemistry
Dr. A. K. Kasthurba NIT Calicut Professor Wins HUDCO Design Award
Dr. Rakesh R. Warier (Assistant Professor, Department of Electrical Engineering) won MoES Grant for Deep Ocean Mission
Dr. Abhineet Prakash (Assistant Professor, Department of Electrical Engineering) got selected in the Top 4 of IEEE PES Outstanding Dissertation Award
NITC Faculty wins INSA Visiting Scientist Award
NITC Faculty Wins Pen America Grant for Translation Work
NIT Calicut Professor Wins HUDCO Design Award
Dr. Indumathi S attends Young Investigators Meet

Dr Minimol M. (Dept. of Chemistry) is honoured by District Women & Child Development Office for her achievements towards science & technology
Dr. C. B. Sobhan receives Dr. K. M. Bahauddin Award
NITC received the first prize for the poster presentation held during state level IIC Regional Meet
NIT Calicut brings accolades - Chess and Badminton Teams show remarkable performance at All India Inter NIT Faculty and Staff Sports Tournament 2023
Dr. Vinay V. Panicker received the award by the Business World Supply Chain Competitiveness  Awards 2023 - in the category: Supply chain education and development excellence, at New Delhi.
Dr. V. Sivasubramanian received the New Code of Education Award 2023 for the Institute of National Importance Category on November 24, 2023, at IIT Delhi.
Dr. Sujith Kumar C. S., Dr. Sudev Das and Prof. S. Jayaraj have received funding under the SPARC scheme in collaboration with NATIONAL TAIWAN UNIVERSITY and NATIONAL CHIAO TUNG UNIVERSITY, Taiwan
An Indian Patent (427649) on “A Method for Determining Jointly the Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) Timing Offset, and Channel Co-Efficients in an Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiple Access (OFDMA) Uplink Wireless Communication System” has been granted to  Prof. Sameer S M and  Thafasal Ijyas V P of Department of Electronics & Communication Engineering
An Indian Patent (433177) on “Superhydrophobic-Omniphobic Membrane for Membrane Distillation with Enhanced Scaling, wetting and fouling Resistance and Preparation Method Thereof” has been granted to  Dr. Noel Jacob and Vivekanandan Sangeetha of Department of Chemical Engineering.
An Indian Patent (429867) on “A Low-Cost Portable Electrochemical Biosensor for Rapid Detection of Endotoxin” has been granted to  Prof. Sandhyarani, Prof. Ravi Varma,  Dr.  Arun, Mr. Tushar and Ms. Haritha.
An Indian Patent (429959) on "Multi–Mode Friction Stir Welding Tool" has been awarded to  Prof. R. Manu, Dr. Jayadeep and Mr Renju Mohan of the Department of Mechanical Engineering.
Dr. Vinod E Madhavan, Assistant Professor, SMSE, is selected for the  Dr. D.C. Pavate Memorial Visiting Fellowship In Cambridge
Dr. Noel Jacob Kaleekal, Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemical Engineering,NIT Calicut, has won the ‘Kerala State Young Scientist Award-2022’
AIINIT Faculty-Staff Sports Champions

Dr. Suchithra TV, Associate Professor  School of Biotechnology has been granted a patent entitled' 'Novel 4-diphenylamino 3-iodo coumarin and a method for its isolation'
Four faculty members of NITC - figured in the Stanford University's list of Top 2% most influential Indian Scientists for the year 2020.
Dr. M K Ravi Varma won the "Dr. S Vasudev Award 2021" for the Best Science Research Scheme project funded by Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE)
Dr. Sathidevi P S, Deputy Director and Professor in ECED Department, NIT Calicut, is selected for the Prof. K M Bahauddin Award for the distinguished engineering teacher of Kerala State.
Dr. C B Sobhan, Professor, SMSE, has been nominated as the Joint Secretary of the prestigious Indian Society of Heat and Mass and Transfer (ISHMT). Earlier he was member of the Executive Committee.
Dr. A P Shashikala was awarded The "Outstanding Woman Structural Engineer of The Year 2020" instituted by The Indian Association Of Structural Engineers, in July 2021.
Dr. N Sandhyarani, Professor, School of Materials Science and Engineering, won the prestigious SERB POWER FELLOWSHIP from the Department Of Science and Technology, Govt. of India
Dr. Kumaravel S Assistant Professor, Department of Electrical Engineering awarded “Young Faculty Research Fellowship” under Visvesvaraya PhD Scheme, Ministry of Electronics & IT, India
Technology to convert waste plastic into fuel by Dr. Lisa Sreejith from Department of Chemistry
Dr. Hanas T for the great achievement.  Dr. Hanas received Sudarshan Bhat Memorial prize from IIT Madras for the best PhD thesis in Metallurgical and Materials Engineering Department for the year 2018

Paper presented by Mr Anup Johnson, Research Scholar and Dr A. Shaija, Associate Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering won the BEST PAPER AWARD
Dr. Aruna, Faculty in School of Management Sciences, won third prize for her paper “Performance Evaluation in Indian Microfinance Context"
Dr Kasthurba A K is selected for the SAH2016 Travel grant award constituted by Society of Architecture Historians (SAH)
Pradeepmon T.G., R. Sridharan and Vinay V. Panicker has been adjudged as the Best Paper presented at AMTE organized by M. A. College of Engineering, Kothamangalam, February 8 – 9, 2016.
Mr. P.V. Manu was conferred with the BEST PAPER AWARD on the occasion of the 4th National Convention on Hydrogen Energy and Advanced Materials (NCHEAM’15)

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
May 20, 2025
NIT Calicut Joins the Prestigious HERITAGE Network
The National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) is pleased to announce its induction into the HERITAGE Network, a distinguished consortium dedicated to enhancing academic cooperation between India and Europe in the domains of Engineering, Science, Design, and Technology.
This significant milestone was formalized during the General Assembly of the Indian and European Universities Consortium, held on 14 May 2025 at TU Dresden, Germany. NIT Calicut was represented by a high-level delegation led by Director Prof. Prasad Krishna, along with senior officials overseeing the institute's international relations and strategic collaborations.
During the Assembly, Director Prof. Prasad Krishna presented a comprehensive overview of NITC’s academic and research profile, emphasizing the institute’s commitment to global partnerships, interdisciplinary excellence, and innovation-led growth. Following a competitive selection and deliberation process, NITC was unanimously elected as a member of the HERITAGE Network.
The HERITAGE Network is a premier Indo-European academic platform that:
Facilitates academic exchange and collaborative research between Indian and European higher education institutions
Supports the development of joint educational programs in Engineering, Technology, and related fields
Hosts annual scientific and technical workshops on key areas of mutual interest
Acts as a policy advisory body to promote and sustain global higher education collaborations
NIT Calicut’s inclusion in the HERITAGE Network marks a pivotal advancement in its internationalization strategy. The institute looks forward to contributing to shared academic and research goals, participating in collaborative programs, and co-creating impactful initiatives with partner institutions.
This membership strengthens NITC’s global footprint and reinforces its vision to be a hub of excellence in education, research, and innovation on the international stage.

PROFILE
Bioscience and Engineering
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2009
Head of the Department:
Dr. Baiju G. Nair
CONTACT
Email id:
hodbiotech@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495 228 5451
Mailing Address
Department of Bioscience and Engineering, National Institute of Technology Calicut, Calicut-Mukkam Road, Kattangal - 673601 Kozhikode, Kerala.
Admissions open: M.Tech Bioengineering
;
Application link:
Click HERE !
The Department of Bioscience and Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Calicut is committed to excellence in education, research, and innovation in the rapidly evolving fields of biosciences and bioengineering. Established with the aim of addressing global challenges through biotechnology, the department has grown to become a hub of multidisciplinary research and academic excellence.
Vision
To be globally recognized in biotechnology education and research leading to excellent and innovative graduates contributing to their profession and society
Mission
To bring about well qualified biotechnology graduates with utmost learning in basic and advanced concepts in different fields of biotechnology.
Equip the students with recent technical, practical and managerial skills to excel in industry, higher studies and entrepreneurship.
Promote research in health science, agriculture as well as basic life science using cutting edge technology for the betterment of society
Placement Brochure: Click
HERE!!
Academic Programs:
-
Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Biotechnology
: A comprehensive undergraduate program that combines the principles of biology and engineering, preparing students for careers in biotechnology, pharmaceuticals, healthcare, and related fields. The curriculum is designed to provide a strong foundation in molecular biology, bioprocess engineering, bioinformatics, and more, with opportunities for hands-on research and industry exposure.
-
Master of Technology (M.Tech) in Bioengineering
:*An advanced program that delves into the application of engineering principles to biological systems. The M.Tech program emphasizes research and innovation, equipping students with the skills and knowledge required to excel in areas such as tissue engineering, biomaterials, and medical devices. Graduates are well-prepared for roles in research and development, academia, and the biotechnology industry.
Research Focus:
Our faculty members are actively engaged in pioneering research in several cutting-edge areas, including but not limited to:
- Microbiology: Exploring microbial diversity, physiology, and applications in environmental and industrial biotechnology.
- Cancer Biology: Investigating molecular mechanisms of cancer, developing novel diagnostics and therapeutic strategies.
- Bioinformatics: Utilizing computational tools to analyze biological data, advancing personalized medicine, and understanding complex biological systems.
- Bioprocess Technology: Innovating sustainable processes for the production of biofuels, biopharmaceuticals, and other bio-based products.
- Neuroscience: Understanding the nervous system at molecular, cellular, and systems levels, with implications for treating neurological disorders.
- Bioengineering: Developing medical devices, biomaterials, and technologies for improving human health.
- Tissue Engineering: Creating artificial tissues and organs for transplantation and disease modeling.
- Molecular Biology: Deciphering the molecular basis of biological processes, with applications in genetics, genomics, and biotechnology.
Industry and Academic Collaborations
We maintain strong collaborations with leading academic institutions, research organizations, and industries both in India and abroad. These partnerships enable us to stay at the forefront of bioscience and bioengineering research, and provide our students with opportunities for internships, joint research projects, and exposure to real-world challenges.
Outreach and Engagement
The department is committed to engaging with the broader community through various outreach activities, including workshops, seminars, and conferences. We also actively participate in national and international collaborations, contributing to the global dialogue on biosciences and bioengineering.
Future Goals
As we look to the future, the Department of Bioscience and Engineering aims to expand its research capabilities, strengthen industry ties, and continue to provide an exceptional educational experience that prepares our graduates to become leaders in their fields. We are dedicated to driving innovation and making a meaningful impact on society through the advancement of biosciences and bioengineering.

Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Electric Vehicle Engineering
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Nikhil Sasidharan, Dr. Shreelakshmi M P
CONTACT
Email id:
chair.ceve@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
+91-495-2286308
Mailing Address
Centre for Electric Vehicle Engineering
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
NIT Campus P.O 673 601,
Kozhikode, India
Centre for Electric Vehicle Engineering (CEVE) at NIT Calicut is dedicated to serve as a specialized Ecosystem for driving innovation, entrepreneurship, research and development, besides training and certification in the realm of e-mobility. The center is a one-of-a-kind academic-industry partnership, positioning NIT Calicut as a pioneering hub for cutting-edge research in Electric Vehicle (EV) and its associated technology. This center functions as a focal point for pioneering advancements in electric vehicle technology, encompassing crucial aspects like battery/ ultracapacitor technologies, microgrid and charging infrastructure, and novel motor designs and motor control algorithms etc. The centre also facilitates patents, renders courses for upskilling, and involves in promoting knowledge and awareness in Green technology.
Vision
Fostering sustainable transportation for global future generations
Mission
To develop state of the art sustainable transportation by fostering innovation, conducting impactful research, and disseminating knowledge in collaboration with academia and industry and enhance efficiency, affordability, and environmental impact in order to contribute to the global transition towards an energy-efficient transportation ecosystem.
Objective
To create and foster a hub for Research, Innovation, Skill Development, Entrepreneurship for Green Mobility.
To provide real time training for the campus recruits in EV domain.
To conduct ground breaking research in EV and associated technologies in collaboration with different stakeholders.
To test and certify the technologies used in e-mobility
To offer M-Tech and PhD program at the center to facilitate multi-disciplinary research in EV technologies
Research Area
Battery/ultracapacitor management system
Cell level design of battery pack
Novel motor and controller design for EV drives.
EMC/EMI mitigation/ filter design
Mechanical Vibration analysis of the EV drives
Thermal management of Battery/drive system
Design of Microgrid/ charging infrastructure
Machine learning based fault detection
Novel design of multilevel drive converters
Novel topologies of high gain DC/ DC converters
Structural analysis of EV drives
Major highlights
Easy access to NITC campus by road/ rail/air
Presently 44 faculty members from diverse specialization are associated with the center
More than 50 doctoral researchers, working in EV and associated technologies, are associated with the Centre.
High-end laboratory dedicated for EV drive research and development
Access to all major laboratory facilities at NITC, including the dedicated research facility for research on High voltage EV drive, in collaboration with TATA Elxsi, worth 1 crore.
Associated with the Centre
TATA ELXSI.
National Institute of Ocean Technology
C-Electric Pvt Ltd
Kerala State Electricity Board
Kochi Metro Rail Ltd
C-MET
Hella Motors
IIT Bombay
IIT Hyderabad
Virginia Tech
Asian Institute of Technology, Thailand

Oct 02, 2024
NIT Calicut Observes Swachhata Hi Seva Campaign
NIT Calicut successfully concluded its Swachhata Hi Seva campaign, the nationwide initiative aimed at promoting cleanliness and environmental awareness. The institute organised a series of events and initiatives over the last few weeks, starting 14th September and culminating in the Swachh Bharat Diwas celebrations on October 2, on the occasion of Gandhi Jayanti.
Professor Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut, inaugurated the campaign and administered a Swachhta Pledge to the institute community, including students, faculty, and staff. The campaign, observed from September 14 to October 1 under the theme "Swabhav Swachhata-Sanskaar Swachhata" focused on fostering a culture of cleanliness and environmental responsibility.
Various activities were undertaken during the campaign, such as weed and plastic waste removal, awareness drives, and collaborative efforts between NITC Community, NCC, NSS, faculty and staff. Under the 'Green Campus Initiative, NCC, NSS and the Sanitation/Horticulture staff joined forces to clear weeds from the Residential Campus on 27 September. On 28 September, a health check-up and hygiene awareness seminar was organized for Sanitation Workers by the Health Centre of NITC.

MENTORSHIP
About NITCAMP
How to register
Past statistics
NITCAMP
About NITCAMP
NITCAA Mentoring Program (NITCAMP) is an initiative of the USA chapter of NITCAA. This platform enables NITC students to interact with NITC alumni experts in various domains, to receive guidance regarding career development, higher studies, and other technical/professional needs. NITCAMP was launched in 2017 and has gained wide acceptance from the students. The sixth edition of NITCAMP rolled out in 2022 had more than 200 alumni mentors from around the world. The student community has benefited immensely from their guidance

Innovations in the Media
INNOVATION
Institution Innovation Council (IIC)
National Institute of Technology Calicut, with an aim of fostering entrepreneurship initiatives among the students has established Institution’s Innovation Cell in association with MHRD and the Ministry of HRD’s Innovation Cell (MHRD) in the year 2017-18. The role of IIC would be to identify and nurture technology based innovative startups.
Visit IIC website

PROFILE
Department of Education
OVERVIEW
Established in:
2023
Head of the Department:
Dr. Geetha Gopinath
CONTACT
Email id:
itep@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2285095
Mailing Address
Department of Education, National Institute of Technology, Calicut NIT Campus Post, Kozhikode, Kerala - 673 601.
The Department of Education is one of the newly started departments of the National Institute of Technology Calicut. Department offers the 4-year Integrated Teacher Education Program (ITEP). This BSc-BEd (Secondary level) is offered with Majors in three different disciplines namely Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in line with the mandate of the National Education Policy 2020 and subsequent initiatives by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE). 4 Year ITEP will be open to all students who choose teaching as a profession after secondary, by choice. Options to exit with a three year BSc degree in Physics, Chemistry or Mathematics depending on the choice of Major in the first semester itself is also available.

NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
Mar 07, 2025
Global Conference on Decarbonising India (GCDI’25)
The Global Conference on Decarbonizing India (GCDI’25) was officially inaugurated today at Aryabhatta Hall, NIT Calicut, bringing together global experts, policymakers, and industry leaders to explore innovative pathways toward a sustainable, low-carbon future.
The inaugural session began with a solemn prayer, followed by a welcome address from Dr. A. Shaija, Chair of GCDI’25 and Chairperson of the Centre for Clean Energy and Circular Economy (C-CECE) at NIT Calicut. The ceremonial lighting of the lamp, performed by all invited dignitaries, symbolised a collective commitment to sustainable energy solutions.
In his Presidential Address, Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut, highlighted the institution's dedication to fostering collaborations that drive impactful research and innovation in decarbonisation and clean energy technologies. The Chief Guest, Shri Nitin Gadkari, Hon'ble Union Cabinet Minister for Road Transport and Highways, joined virtually to deliver the Inaugural Address. He outlined India’s policy initiatives and strategic vision to achieve carbon neutrality in the transportation and infrastructure sectors.
The session also featured insightful addresses from distinguished Guests of Honour. Dr. Ranjith Krishna Pai, Senior Director, Climate, Energy, and Sustainable Technology (CEST) at the Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India, discussed the opportunities available through DST projects for fostering indigenous hydrogen technologies. Dr. P.V. Aravind from the University of Groningen, Netherlands, spoke about the development of the hydrogen value chain through publicprivate partnerships, emphasising the importance of collaboration in the energy transition. Dr. Naomi Boness, Managing Director of the Natural Gas Initiative at Stanford University, reinforced the significance of the conference in the global energy landscape and the urgency of decarbonisation. Mr. P. Jayadevan, Retired Executive Director of Indian Oil Corporation Ltd. (IOCL), underscored the need for India to accelerate its energy transition and develop a comprehensive energy index to guide policy and industry strategies.
The inaugural session concluded with a Vote of Thanks by Dr. Panneerselvam R, Co-Chair of GCDI’25 and Chairperson of the Centre for Sustainable Technologies (CST) at NIT Calicut. He expressed gratitude to the esteemed guests, the organising committee, and participants for their contributions to the success of the conference.
GCDI’25 will host multiple technical sessions, industry panels, and exhibitions, showcasing pioneering research and technological advancements in sustainable energy. The conference underscores NIT Calicut’s role as a hub for sustainable innovation and climate action. The event is jointly hosted by NITC’s Centre for Clean Energy and Circular Economy (C-CECE) and Centre for Sustainable Technologies (CST).

Mridula C V Selected for CHARPAK Summer Training 2025
IndiaAI Mission fellowships for a Student of NIT Calicut
Amal Faris of NIT Calicut has been recommended by the 33 Service Selection Board, Selection Centre Central, Bhopal
Indian Patent (No: 555375) has been granted for an invention entitled a scaleable micro-aerial vehicle surveillance system with a counter abstraction model and method of operation
Sankeerth J I (of Dept. of Electrical Engineering) is qualified for the prestigious All India Inter University Karate Tournament in the 67KG Category
NITC students are selected as IndiaAI Fellows by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology((MeitY)), Government of India
NITC Students remarkable achievements in Football and Lawn Tennis Team in the All India Inter NIT 2024-25
NITC Student represented the Kozhikode District Cricket Association in the Kerala Cricket Association North Zone U-23
Mathworks Minidrone Competition India : 2nd Prize
NITC student wins the NIUA-NMCG Best Thesis National Award for her work on Nagpur city
Six cadets of NCC NITC selected for IGC Camp
Ms. Neha Sathish (of B.tech Biotechnology - 2021 joining) has secured the prestigious DAAD-wise scholarship for pursuing her internship at the Technical University of Darmstadt, Germany
NITC Students Shine at Dhwani ‘24
Men’s Volleyball Team Bags Inter-NIT Championship
Akhila K., an M.Sc. Chemistry student at NITC, has won the prestigious Chief Minister's Vidyarthi Prathibha Award
NITC bags laurels in Inter- NIT athletic meet
NIT Calicut won championship in Inter-NIT Kho Kho Tournament 2024
NITC students grab medals in All India Inter-NIT Sports Tournament
Best outgoing student award for Mr. Likith
NITC has emerged champions at the Kozhikode district Senior Handball Championship
NIT Calicut Mime Team tops at Mood Indigo
NIT Calicut Research Scholar Awarded Commonwealth Split-Site Doctoral Scholarship for UK Study
Nongmaithem Sagar awarded as best powerlifter of All India Inter NIT Power sports championship 2023 held at NIT Durgapu
NIT Calicut shines in All India Inter-NIT handball, kabaddi and basketball tournaments

Best Outgoing Female Researcher Award 2023
NITC team wins accolades at the e-Yantra Robotics competition at IIT Bombay

Selected for sponsored thesis competition NIUA, NMCG
NITC is proud of our students achievements in interNIT sports competitions

Best Emerging Researcher Award
Selection of NCC cadets to the national camp

Thematic Centres
PROFILE
Centre for Cultural and Art Relations (CCAR)
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Mahesh Kumar
CONTACT
Mailing Address
Dr. Mahesh Kumar
Chairperson
Centre for Cultural and Art Relations
NIT Calicut
673601
It goes without saying that creativity and innovation are indispensable qualities in the engineering field. Even though engineering students are introduced to the engineering design process, this does not necessarily translate into the development of creative ability that can be applied more broadly. Introducing performing arts such as dance, music, drawing, painting, and drama as an integral part of engineering curriculum and encouraging students to learn may help to develop and train engineers to be creative. Furthermore, practicing performing arts may improve engineers' discipline, dedication, focus, and confidence. The ability to innovate and communicate, which can be developed through music and the arts, is what leads to scientific excellence. Indeed, ancient Indian universities like Takshashila, Vallabhi, Vikramshila, and Nalanda used holistic approaches in education system, combining arts, communication, vocational skills with science and medicine.
Hobbies are an excellent way to deal with mental health issues, and significantly improve the overall fitness and well-being. Engineers are hard workers, so taking up a new hobby in the form of arts, dance, music, or drama may be just what they need to relieve stress. Furthermore, hobbies are one of the defining factors that employers now use to determine personality type because they indicate a well-rounded nature of a candidate. As a result, learning performing arts not only improves the overall fitness and well-being but also increases employability.
Following the new National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, the emphasis on multidisciplinary learning, as well as the need to nurture creative disciplines such as classical and folk arts in technical institutes, has become imperative. Engineers benefit from creative fields such as performing arts because they help them think from a broader perspective and boost their confidence.
The Centre for Cultural and Art Relations (CCAR) was established with the primary goal of making engineering education more multidisciplinary and improving students' learning experiences through the incorporation of fine and performing arts into the curriculum, both of which are clearly aligned with the NEP2020 vision statements. The centre aims to provide holistic education with innovation and style by combining arts and engineering, which may give our young minds a new way of seeing, perceiving, and interacting with their world, leaving an indelible impression on their senses. It aspires to be a hub of art in practice and discourse within NITC. Public event programmes, workshops, research, and pedagogy are all expected to help build a community of artistic-engineers interested in the intersections of art, culture, and technology. The centre strives to collaborate with cultural and artistic talents at the regional, national, and international levels in order to build bridges with the community in novel and exciting ways.
Vision
Encourage young minds to appreciate and learn traditional performing and creative arts forms in order to develop concentration, coordination, confidence, and creativity, all of which are enablers for excellence in science and engineering.
The center strives to create a holistic educational system by integrating Indian and Foreign fine and performing arts into engineering curricula.
The center serves as a platform for collaboration between the arts and engineering fields through a series of courses that will generate new educational opportunities and encourage creative teamwork among engineering and art students, faculty, staff, as well as visiting artists and scholars.
The center's goal is to blur the boundaries between disciplines and give students the freedom to exercise their choice and pursue their hobbies and dreams without sacrificing academic careers.
Mission
The center provides micro-credit courses in areas connecting music, fine arts and technology apart from lectures and workshops.
To equip students with the creative and intellectual tools necessary for success in the engineering and science.
To instill in students the importance of valuing, comprehending, and questioning traditional concepts.
To provide world-class and multicultural arts experiences for both students and community members.
To develop a programme that encourages and supports collaborations between artists and engineers to stimulate creative teamwork and promote research and innovation.
Additionally, the center encourages enrolment from participants from all over the world who are interested in learning about the unique aspects of Indian culture, music, and fine arts.
Assist in the advancement of scientific research and innovation by combining engineering technologies (AI and ML) with creative and performing arts.
OVERVIEW
Chairperson:
Dr. Mahesh Kumar

IMPORTANT NOTIFICATIONS
2024/02 Group A Administrative positions
NIT Calicut shines in national rankings, continues legacy of excellence
NITC continues stellar placement record
Close
About NITC
National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC) is one of the 31 institutions of national importance set up by an Act of Parliament namely the ‘National Institute of technology Act 2007’, and is fully funded by the Government of India. The mandate of the Institute is to provide higher technical education and conduct research in the various branches of Engineering, Science, Technology and Management. Originally established in 1961 as a Regional Engineering College (REC), it was transformed into a National Institute of Technology in 2002. The Institute offers bachelors, masters and doctoral degree programs in Engineering, Science, Technology and Management. With its proactive collaborations with a multitude of research organizations, academic institutions and industries, the institute has set a new style for its functioning under the NIT regime.
Set in a picturesque landscape at the foothills of the Western Ghats, NIT Calicut is located about 22 kilometres north-east of Calicut city in the state of Kerala, India. It stretches over a length of about 1.5 kilometres along the Calicut-Mukkam road, extending over an area of approximately 120 hectares. Calicut, also known as Kozhikode, located in the Malabar region of Kerala State, found a place in world history with the discovery of a sea route to India in 1498 by the Portuguese navigator Vasco Da Gama. Basking in the idyllic setting of the Arabian Sea on the west and the proud peaks of the Wayanad hills on the east, Calicut is known for its serene beaches, lush green countryside, historic sites, calm backwaters, wildlife sanctuaries, rivers and waterfalls.
Being a fully residential institution, the campus houses academic buildings, research labs, hostels, residences and other amenities among its infrastructure. The Institute is presently offering 11 UG programmes and 29 PG programmes including MBA. Doctoral level research has remarkably increased in recent times with a substantial increase in the volume of research papers and patents produced.
Many transformations in academic institutions become imperative for embracing the changes that happen by the rule of the nature and also for absorbing the results of the pursuits of the humanity in the areas of knowledge and skill. The case of converting the premier Regional Engineering Colleges (RECs) to the new brand of institutions of national importance was with such a purpose. The enactment of the NIT Act in 2007 has fuelled the growth of the new line of institutions of national importance named as National Institutes of Technology (NITs) and are being built to improve the social equity in high quality education in science, technology, engineering and management. The primary purpose of national integration envisaged for the RECs has been upheld in the formation of NITs too, but a new focus of research was added to the mandate.
Originally established in 1961 as a Regional Engineering College (REC), National Institute of Technology Calicut has begun its triumphant journey with a new spirit in 2002, with the new name and a fresh mandate. This had helped the institute in broadening the spectrum of services to the humanity; Institute now offers Bachelors, Masters and Doctoral degree programmes in Engineering, Science, Technology and Management.
The region of Malabar that hosts the Institute is the northern part of the State of Kerala and is a destination for ecotourism. The region is fast developing and is vibrant in all spheres of human life; there is no smoke and choke of heavy industries. Local business, small scale industries and the overseas jobs of the local population combined with the revolutionary practices of land reforms have resulted in a uniform distribution of development across the region. Perhaps, the entire State of Kerala has the advantage of these enabling factors. This development has helped in removing the disparities between city and the rural region. The threat of global warming could not yet steal the natural beauty of the landscape. Anyone can enjoy this from the pinnacles of the hills that mark the undulating topography. Malabar is historically known for the spices and the trading with the Middle East. As an institution of higher education and research, NIT Calicut cannot alienate itself from this growth pattern but has taken the lead in the regional development through many proactive measures as a friend, philosopher and guide. Faculty is posed with many challenges because of the princely treatment the institution gets from the public. So, the infrastructure for testing, research and development is undergoing continuous improvement.
Redefining the goals and putting thrust on socially relevant subjects has enabled the Institute to produce ready-to-employ graduates and the value added research outputs with sustained quality and excellence. Notwithstanding the challenges that get opened up in terms resource crunch, lukewarm industrial supports, the institute is marching ahead and has marked its position as the 23rd one among technical institutions and third in the area of architecture in the year 2020. Campus is catering to the 6500 strong student population with a variety of services and state of the art education. Our roadmap is to reach the top and work with the best in the class institutions that are globally well known. We believe that excellence is a journey and not a destination.
Institute is indebted to the Government of India, Ministry of Education, Kerala State Government, the local administration, industries, funding agencies, alumni and all other stakeholders for the grand support in its march towards excellence.

More...
VISION
International standing of the highest calibre.
MISSION
To develop high quality technical education and personnel with a sound footing on basic engineering principles, technical and managerial skills, innovative research capabilities, and exemplary professional conduct to lead and to use technology for the progress of mankind, adapting themselves to changing technological environment with the highest ethical values as the inner strength.

Sports and Games
Announcements
SPORTS & GAMES
The 'Center for Student Activities and Sports' (Formerly Department of Physical Education) oversees the sports activities at NITC.
The National Institute of Technology, Calicut is proud to offer a well-rounded athletic program and a vibrant sports community to its students. Our sports department promotes an active and healthy lifestyle through a variety of sports activities. NITC offers sports events like Athletics, Swimming, Football, Basketball, Volleyball, Badminton, Cricket, Hockey, Handball, Kabaddi, Kho-Kho, Table tennis, Chess and Power sports. Besides these competitive sports, we offer yoga facilities to our students for a healthier lifestyle. In order to help our students achieve their full potential, our coaches are highly experienced and knowledgeable in their respective sports.
Furthermore, we have the necessary facilities to match the level of coaching. To ensure that our students have the best experience possible, we offer state-of-the-art courts, swimming pools, and gymnasiums.
We offer our students the chance to participate in various tournaments throughout the year, including National Championships, State Championships, All India Inter University Tournaments, All India Inter NIT Tournaments, Inter-Branch, Inter-Hostel, and Inter-College events held during our annual festivals, Ragam & Tathva.

PROFILE
Centre for Computational Modelling and Simulation (CCMS)
OVERVIEW
Faculty in Charge:
Dr. Raghu Chatanathodi
CONTACT
Email id:
ccmsadmin@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2285600
Mailing Address
Centre for Computaional Modelling And Simulations (CCMS),
National Institute of Technology Calicut,
NIT Campus, Kozhikode, India P.O 673 601
Centre for Computational Modelling and Simulation (CCMS) aims to promote and support computational modelling as a mainstream research activity amongst the faculty and researchers of NIT Calicut. A High Performance Computing (HPC) Cluster and NVIDIA DGX Station purchased under HEFA are the major computing facility under this centre. The facility consists of 31 CPU nodes and 2 GPU nodes. The NVIDIA DGX Station has four V100 GPU accelerators. The facility is mainly for developing and running parallel codes for research purposes. The HPC system installed has been ranked as the 38th in the list of top 100 computing machines in India, in January 2021, a list maintained by CDAC, Bangalore https://topsc.cdacb.in/topsc.php/filterdetails?slug=January2021)

NITC HOSTELS

OVERVIEW
National Institute of Technology Calicut Hostels
HOSTEL MAIN OFFICE
Chairperson - Council of Wardens:
Dr. K. Haribabu
Email id:
chairperson.cow@nitc.ac.in
Contact no:
0495-2286957
Assistant Registrar (Hostel Administration):
Mr. Vivek K V
Email id:
arhostels@nitc.ac.in
Warden- Hostel Office:
Dr. Allen Anilkumar (MED)
Email id:
officewarden@nitc.ac.in
The campus houses eleven hostels for boys and three hostels for girls, collectively accommodating a vibrant community of 6800 students. Seven out of the eleven boys' hostels offer single or double occupancy accommodations, while the remaining four provide triple or quadruple occupancy. The boys' hostels have a total capacity of around 4800 students. For the girls' hostels, some rooms are designated for single or double occupancy, while the rest are available for triple or quadruple occupancy, accommodating approximately 2000 students.
Each hostel has common rooms and entertainment areas, offering access to newspapers, magazines, televisions, and indoor sports/games facilities. Hostels provide guest rooms for visitors and family members of residents at a nominal fee. All hostels ensure a 24/7 supply of water and electricity, along with round-the-clock internet and computer access. The ladies hostels boast additional amenities such as a gym, a sewing and tailoring center, a library, an indoor sports center, a DTP center, and a night canteen. The men’s hostels feature an outdoor gym, synthetic volleyball and basketball courts, a cricket net practice area, a badminton court, and a dedicated sports complex within the Mega Hostel. Spacious dining areas cater to the diverse culinary preferences of the residents, supported by 16 messes, two of which are exclusively vegetarian. Other amenities include laundry services, extension counters of the NITC cooperative store, and photocopy centers.
Hostel activities are supervised by wardens and caretakers during the day, with attendants available around the clock. Each hostel has a council comprising the warden, caretaker, and student representatives.  This council is responsible for managing the hostel's facilities and overseeing its continuous development. CCTV monitoring, supported by the dedicated efforts of security personnel and housekeeping staff, ensures a secure and impeccably clean living environment.
To enhance the hostel experience and promote academic and personal growth, various sports and cultural events, such as inter-hostel and intra-hostel tournaments, are organized annually. The hostels at NITC provide a holistic living experience that supports the professional and personal development of the students, creating a nurturing and dynamic community.

UNDERGRADUATE ADMISSIONS
B.Tech and B.Arch Admissions - Overview
Admission to the B. Tech. and B. Arch degree programmes of NIT Calicut is based on the performance in the Joint Entrance Examination, JEE (Main) - Paper 1 and Paper 2 respectively. Reservation of seats to various categories, such as SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PWD, will be followed as per Govt. of India norms. For information on JEE (Main), please visit
www.jeemain.nic.in
.
There is no need to apply separately to each NIT. The Joint Seat Allocation Authority (JoSAA) and the Central Seat Allocation Board (CSAB), constituted by the Ministry of Education (Erstwhile Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD)), Government of India, will conduct and supervise the process of admission to the various NITs through centralized online counselling process. More details about the admission process will be made available at the websites of JoSAA (
https://josaa.nic.in
) and CSAB (
https://csab.nic.in
), from time to time.
Academic details about the various undergraduate programmes offered by the Institute are available at the following link of NITC website:
https://nitc.ac.in/ug-programmes
A limited number of seats (145 seats on supernumerary basis) are available for Foreign Nationals / Persons of Indian origin (PIOs) / Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) / Overseas Citizens of India (OCI) under the ‘Direct Admission of Students Abroad (DASA)’ Scheme of Ministry of Education (MoE), Government of India (earlier known as Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD)). 1/3rd of the DASA seats are reserved for the Children of Indian Workers in the Gulf Countries (CIWG). Those seeking admission under the DASA/CIWG scheme may please visit the DASA website (
https://dasanit.org/
)
Joint Entrance Examination (Main)
The Ministry of Education [erstwhile Ministry of Human Resources Development (MHRD)], Government of India (GOI) has established National Testing Agency (NTA) as an independent autonomous and self-sustained premier testing organization under Society Registration Act 1860 for conducting efficient, transparent and international standards tests in order to assess the competency of candidates for admissions to premier higher education institutions.
The Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Human Resource Development, Government of India has entrusted the responsibility of conducting Joint Entrance Examination JEE (Main) to the NTA from 2019 onwards.
Admission criteria to Undergraduate Engineering Programs at NITs, IIITs, other Centrally Funded Technical Institutions (CFTI), Institutions funded by participating State Governments, and other Institutions shall include the performance in the class 12/equivalent qualifying Examination and in the Joint Entrance Examination, JEE (Main). The (B. E. /B. Tech.) of JEE (Main) will also be an eligibility test for the JEE (Advanced), which the candidate has to take if he/she is aspiring for admission to the undergraduate programs offered by the Indian Institute of Technology (IITs).
For further details, please visit:
https://jeemain.nta.nic.in/

ADMINISTRATION
Overview
NIT Calicut is a fully centrally funded, academically autonomous institute of national importance and is governed by the NIT Act 2007. Hierarchically, President of India is the Visitor to the Institute under the Act. The governance structure contains the national Council for NITs as the apex policy making body, while the Institute’s governance is vested with the Board of Governors. Institute’s Senate is the authority in academic matters. Chairman of Board of Governors is appointed by the Visitor. The broad based governance and administrative system given under the NIT Act and First Statutes of NITs 2009 ensures participative decision making and assures Accountability.
The Director is the executive head of the institute. He is appointed by the Ministry of Education (MoE) for a period of five years. Director is a Member in the Board of Governors and the NIT Council and is also the Chairman of the Senate. The management roles of the Institute are looked after bythe Deans (Academic, Planning and Development, Faculty Welfare, Research andConsultancy, Student Welfare, Alumni Affairs and International Relations) and theHeads of the Departments and Schools. Administration wing consists of the Registrar,the Deputy Registrars, Assistant Registrars, other Officers and the Ministerial staff. Executive wing and Maintence wing is consisting of the engineering unit, electrical maintenance unit, water distribution unit, security and sanitation units.
Constitution of the Senate is prescribed by the NIT Act and members are drawn from the faculty of the Institute as well as from outside. Senate is responsible for the academic administration, including introduction and maintenance of academic courses. Subordinate to the Senate, there is a Board of Academic Council (BOAC) headed by the Dean (Academic) and is playing the link role between the Senate and the departments to make appropriate recommendations to the Senate. The student Hostels are administered by the Chief Warden, with the help of a council of Wardens and the Hostel and Mess Committee. Student activities are planned and managed by the elected body of Students Affairs Council, which is overseen by the Dean (Students Welfare) and other faculty coordinators. Central Computing facility, campus Networking facility, Central Instrumentation facility and the Library provide the backbone support for academics and research. Technology Business Incubator is playing the pivotal role in social outreach and the promotion of the startup ecosystem. There are facilities like Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development Centre, Centre for Transportation Research, Design Innovation Centre, Centre for Materials Characterization, Centre for Computational Modeling and Simulation, IPR Cell, etc. to provide the support for R&D with thrust on innovations.

LIFE AT NITC

NITC Multipurpose Cooperative Society
LIFE AT NITC
Faculty Club
The NITC Faculty Club is a social organization within NITC for its faculty community and their families. The primary goal of the club is to provide a platform for members to interact, network, and engage in social and recreational activities. It serves as a community for the faculty members to connect and build friendly relationships outside of their professional roles. The club actively organizes events, activities, and programs such as social gatherings, sports competitions, recreational trips, and cultural events among other things. The NITC Faculty Club is an important component of the NITC culture and helps its members maintain a positive work-life balance

Mar 04, 2025
EKASVA 2025 – Innovation Meet and Patent Expo Kicks Off at NIT Calicut
The highly anticipated EKASVA 2025 – Innovation Meet and Patent Expo was officially inaugurated today at the National Institute of Technology Calicut (NITC). Hosted by the Centre for Innovation, Entrepreneurship, and Incubation (CIEI), the even brought together top industry leaders, innovators, and entrepreneurs from across the nation to discuss groundbreaking advancements in technology and intellectual property.
The event was inaugurated by Mr. Ramanan Ramanathan, Chairman of the National Expert Advisory Council on Technology, Innovation, Incubation, and Entrepreneurship at the Department
of Science and Technology (DST), and Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut. The inauguration also saw the presence of Sri Prakash Shetty, Managing Director of Gasotech and NITC alumnus, along with Sri Nagaraja Parakasam, renowned author and a key leader in the Indian AngelNetwork.
In his inaugural speech, Mr. Ramanan Ramanathan emphasised the critical role of young innovators in driving technological change and societal progress. He urged attendees to push the limits of creativity and come up with innovative solutions to address global challenges. "The future of technology lies in the hands of the youth, and their ideas can lead to meaningful transformations in the world," said Ramanathan.
Prof. Prasad Krishna, Director of NIT Calicut and Chief Patron of EKASVA 2025, followed with a
presidential address that highlighted the importance of startups in shaping the future of India's
economy. He called on the younger generation to shift from being job seekers to job creators. “Startups are not just businesses; they are the foundation of our future economy. Young innovators
have the potential to redefine industries,” he said.
Sri Prakash Shetty, Managing Director of Gasotech, also spoke as the Guest of Honour, stressing
the importance of fostering an entrepreneurial mindset. He underscored that innovation is essential
for overcoming global challenges, adding that an entrepreneurial approach could pave the way for
sustainable solutions.
The inaugural session concluded with heartfelt felicitations presented by Mr. Nagaraja Prakasham,
renowned author and angel investor, and Dr. Sandhya Rani, Dean (R&C) at NITC. Their words of
encouragement were followed by a vote of thanks from Dr. T. J. Sarvothama Jothi, President of the
Institution’s Innovation Council (IIC) at NITC.
EKASVA 2025, which focuses on the development of cutting-edge technologies in sectors such as
AI/ML, Smart Agriculture, Sustainable Technologies, and Smart Mobility, continues tomorrow with a series of discussions, showcases, and networking sessions. These will aim to foster collaboration between innovators and industry leaders, while promoting the importance of intellectual property rights and patenting.
The event will feature an Innovation Showcase, keynote sessions on commercialisation and patents, technology/patent transfer guidance, seed funding for startups, incubation opportunities through TBI and FAB Labs, mentorship programs, startup-investor pitch sessions, and awards recognising outstanding contributions to innovation and entrepreneurship.
With 302 registered participants, the event showcases 71 patents and 63 innovative projects by
various pioneers in the field. Additionally, 9 startups have registered to showcase their work,
reflecting the growing entrepreneurial spirit among the attendees.
EKASVA 2025 is set to be a landmark event in the Indian innovation landscape, offering a platform for startups to gain exposure, mentorship, and crucial funding. Supported by venture capitalists, government officials, and key stakeholders, the event is poised to make a significant contribution to India’s expanding innovation ecosystem and accelerate the country’s technological and entrepreneurial growth.

FACILITY
Health Centre
Welcome to NIT Calicut Health Centre
We are committed to providing quality healthcare services to the students, faculty, staff, and their families at NIT Calicut. Equipped with a dedicated medical team, we aim to ensure the physical and mental well-being of the campus community.
Operating Hours:
Monday to Friday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM
Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM
Sundays and Holidays : 9:00 AM - 11:00 AM
Emergency Services: Available 24/7
Contact Us:
Phone: 0495-2286652
Mobile : 9188925297
Email
:
healthcentre@nitc.ac.in
Location: Beside the NIT CALICUT Cooperative Store, Near REC School NIT Calicut Campus
Amenities
NITC Health Center strives to be the primary health center support for the NITC students, staff and faculty members.
Medical Facilities Outside the Institution
Facility for Direct Payment from Institute for patient treatment is made available at KMCT Medical College Hospital Manassery, Ratnaprabha Dental Hospital Kunnamangalam and Comtrust Eye Hospital Calicut for faculty and staff
24*7 Support
Our doctors are available for emergency consultations 24X7. Apart from the regular OP Timing, you can consult at C1 and C2 quarters in Faculty Residence Area.
Ambulance
We provide 24X7 ambulance support for any emergency.
Phone no :
0495 2286661
Laboratory Facilities
Blood Routine Test:
Complete Blood Cell(CBC) Test,
Blood Sugar Test,Cholesterol Test,
Renal Function Test(RFT)
Liver Function Test(LFT)
Urine Routine Test
Blood Group Test
Calcium Test
Laboratory Timing
Mon-Fri - 8 : 30 AM to 4 : 00 PM
Sat - 9 : 00 AM to 1 : 00 PM

Wg Cdr Dr Bensy Stephen

Paramedical Staff
Nursing Staff: Amritha M
Support Staff: 3
Pharmacist:  Devarkonda Venkata Ramana
Health Inspector: 1
Lab Technician: 1
Services
Offered
Medical Services
General consultations for common illnesses.
Preventive health check-ups.
Management of chronic conditions such as diabetes and hypertension.
Pharmacy
In-house pharmacy with essential medicines.
Prescription refills available during working hours.
Diagnostic Services
On-site laboratory for blood tests. The tests done include CBC, ESR,RFT, LFT, LIPID PROFILE, SERUM CALCIUM
Wellness Programs
Mental health counselling by trained psychologist.
Health Camps and health awareness sessions.
For Students
Health Insurance
All students are covered under a group health insurance policy that provides cashless treatment at empaneled hospitals on Admission. Visit the insurance desk at the Academics for details.
Medical Leave
Students requiring medical leave must consult the doctor at the health center and submit a medical certificate for approval.
For FACULTY AND STAFFS
Cashless Facility is available for Treatment in KMCT hospital, Rethnaprabha Dental Hospital, Comtrust Charitable Eye Hospital on Referral from Institute Health Centre.
Medical Reimbursement is Applicable as per CS (MA) Rules.
Emergency Services
Security Front Gate : 0495 2286661
FAQs
What is the consultation fee?
Consultation is free for students, faculty, and staff.
Does the health center provide inpatient services?
Currently, we do not have inpatient facilities but can provide first-aid and referrals.
Can I get a referral for specialized treatment outside?
Yes, our doctors can provide referrals for treatment at empaneled hospitals.

Jun 09
Workshop on Practical Finite Element Analysis with Ansys-2025
A workshop on "Practical Finite Element Analysis with Ansys-2025" is planned in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, NIT Calicut from June 9-13, 2025. The objective of the workshop is to bridge the gap between theories of finite element analysis and the solution of the problems using finite element software, particularly Ansys. The event is scheduled in Hybrid mode, but it is recommended to attend offline for better hands-on training experience in the software. Those who could not attend offline, can attend online. Registration of the course can be done with the following link.
https://forms.gle/A4py6He9gAtLdnVAA
